arguments.content:
---
name: windows-system-hardware
description: Comprehensive Windows system and hardware configuration skill for inventory, analysis, diagnostics, and configuration. Use when working with CPU, GPU, memory, storage, network adapters, BIOS/UEFI, drivers, device management, performance monitoring, or system specifications. Triggers on hardware inventory, system info, device enumeration, driver operations, health monitoring, benchmark data, or any Windows hardware configuration task.
---

# Windows System & Hardware Configuration

## Quick Start

```powershell
# Get comprehensive system overview
.\scripts\Get-SystemInventory.ps1 -Full

# Hardware-specific queries
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category CPU
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category GPU -Detailed
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category Storage -HealthCheck
```

## Core Capabilities

### System Inventory
- Complete hardware enumeration via WMI/CIM
- BIOS/UEFI configuration detection
- Motherboard and chipset identification
- TPM status and capabilities

### Hardware Categories
- **CPU**: Cores, threads, cache, frequency, features (AVX, virtualization)
- **GPU**: Adapters, VRAM, driver versions, display outputs
- **Memory**: DIMMs, speed, type, slots, capacity
- **Storage**: Drives, partitions, health (SMART), volumes
- **Network**: Adapters, MAC, IP config, link speed
- **Audio**: Devices, drivers, endpoints
- **USB**: Controllers, connected devices, power

### Driver Management
- Enumerate installed drivers with versions
- Export driver packages for backup
- Identify unsigned/problematic drivers
- Driver update availability check

### Health & Diagnostics
- SMART data for storage devices
- Temperature sensors (when available)
- Event log analysis for hardware errors
- Performance counter baselines

## Script Reference

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `Get-SystemInventory.ps1` | Full system hardware report |
| `Get-HardwareInfo.ps1` | Category-specific hardware data |
| `Get-DriverInventory.ps1` | Driver enumeration and analysis |
| `Export-DriverPackage.ps1` | Backup drivers to folder |
| `Get-StorageHealth.ps1` | SMART and disk health |
| `Get-SystemPerformance.ps1` | Performance counters and baselines |
| `Test-HardwareHealth.ps1` | Comprehensive health check |

## Output Formats

All scripts support `-OutputFormat` parameter:
- `Object` (default): PowerShell objects for pipeline
- `JSON`: Structured JSON for APIs/storage
- `HTML`: Formatted report with styling
- `CSV`: Tabular export

## Integration Notes

Scripts use CIM cmdlets (preferred) with WMI fallback. Registry queries supplement hardware data where CIM lacks detail. All scripts include:
- Elevation detection with graceful degradation
- Error handling with actionable messages
- Progress indication for long operations
- Verbose logging via `-Verbose`

## References

- [WMI Classes Reference](references/wmi-classes.md) - Common WMI/CIM classes
- [Registry Hardware Paths](references/registry-paths.md) - Hardware registry locations
- [SMART Attributes](references/smart-attributes.md) - Storage health interpretation

---
name: windows-system-hardware
description: Comprehensive Windows system and hardware configuration skill for inventory, analysis, diagnostics, and configuration. Use when working with CPU, GPU, memory, storage, network adapters, BIOS/UEFI, drivers, device management, performance monitoring, or system specifications. Triggers on hardware inventory, system info, device enumeration, driver operations, health monitoring, benchmark data, or any Windows hardware configuration task.
---

# Windows System & Hardware Configuration

## Quick Start

```powershell
# Get comprehensive system overview
.\scripts\Get-SystemInventory.ps1 -Full

# Hardware-specific queries
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category CPU
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category GPU -Detailed
.\scripts\Get-HardwareInfo.ps1 -Category Storage -HealthCheck
```

## Core Capabilities

### System Inventory
- Complete hardware enumeration via WMI/CIM
- BIOS/UEFI configuration detection
- Motherboard and chipset identification
- TPM status and capabilities

### Hardware Categories
- **CPU**: Cores, threads, cache, frequency, features (AVX, virtualization)
- **GPU**: Adapters, VRAM, driver versions, display outputs
- **Memory**: DIMMs, speed, type, slots, capacity
- **Storage**: Drives, partitions, health (SMART), volumes
- **Network**: Adapters, MAC, IP config, link speed
- **Audio**: Devices, drivers, endpoints
- **USB**: Controllers, connected devices, power

### Driver Management
- Enumerate installed drivers with versions
- Export driver packages for backup
- Identify unsigned/problematic drivers
- Driver update availability check

### Health & Diagnostics
- SMART data for storage devices
- Temperature sensors (when available)
- Event log analysis for hardware errors
- Performance counter baselines

## Script Reference

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `Get-SystemInventory.ps1` | Full system hardware report |
| `Get-HardwareInfo.ps1` | Category-specific hardware data |
| `Get-DriverInventory.ps1` | Driver enumeration and analysis |
| `Export-DriverPackage.ps1` | Backup drivers to folder |
| `Get-StorageHealth.ps1` | SMART and disk health |
| `Get-SystemPerformance.ps1` | Performance counters and baselines |
| `Test-HardwareHealth.ps1` | Comprehensive health check |

## Output Formats

All scripts support `-OutputFormat` parameter:
- `Object` (default): PowerShell objects for pipeline
- `JSON`: Structured JSON for APIs/storage
- `HTML`: Formatted report with styling
- `CSV`: Tabular export

## Integration Notes

Scripts use CIM cmdlets (preferred) with WMI fallback. Registry queries supplement hardware data where CIM lacks detail. All scripts include:
- Elevation detection with graceful degradation
- Error handling with actionable messages
- Progress indication for long operations
- Verbose logging via `-Verbose`

## References

- [WMI Classes Reference](references/wmi-classes.md) - Common WMI/CIM classes
- [Registry Hardware Paths](references/registry-paths.md) - Hardware registry locations
- [SMART Attributes](references/smart-attributes.md) - Storage health interpretation

@echo off
title Ultimate PE/RE/Hybrid Builder
color 0B

set ROOT=D:\My-Win[PE][RE]
set OUT=%ROOT%\Output
set WIMS=%OUT%\WIMs
set ISOOUT=%OUT%\ISO

echo ============================================
echo   ULTIMATE PE/RE/HYBRID BUILD AUTOMATION
echo ============================================
echo.

REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Create directories
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Creating required directories...
mkdir "%WIMS%" >nul 2>&1
mkdir "%ISOOUT%" >nul 2>&1
mkdir "%ROOT%\CombinedISO" >nul 2>&1

REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Build WinPE WIM
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Building WinPE image...
dism /Mount-WIM /WimFile:%ROOT%\WinPE\base\boot.wim /index:1 /MountDir:%ROOT%\WinPE\mount

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%ROOT%\Tool-AutoSetup.ps1" -MountDir "%ROOT%\WinPE\mount" -ToolRoot "%ROOT%"

echo [+] Injecting WinPE drivers...
dism /Image:%ROOT%\WinPE\mount /Add-Driver /Driver:%ROOT%\WinPE\drivers\ /Recurse

echo [+] Committing WinPE build...
dism /Unmount-WIM /MountDir:%ROOT%\WinPE\mount /Commit

copy "%ROOT%\WinPE\base\boot.wim" "%WIMS%\WinPE_Custom.wim" /Y


REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Build WinRE WIM
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Building WinRE image...
dism /Mount-WIM /WimFile:%ROOT%\WinRE\base\winre.wim /index:1 /MountDir:%ROOT%\WinRE\mount

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%ROOT%\Tool-AutoSetup.ps1" -MountDir "%ROOT%\WinRE\mount" -ToolRoot "%ROOT%"

echo [+] Injecting WinRE drivers...
dism /Image:%ROOT%\WinRE\mount /Add-Driver /Driver:%ROOT%\WinRE\drivers\ /Recurse

echo [+] Committing WinRE build...
dism /Unmount-WIM /MountDir:%ROOT%\WinRE\mount /Commit

copy "%ROOT%\WinRE\base\winre.wim" "%WIMS%\WinRE_Custom.wim" /Y


REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Build Hybrid WIM
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Building Hybrid image...
dism /Mount-WIM /WimFile:%ROOT%\Hybrid\base\hybrid.wim /index:1 /MountDir:%ROOT%\Hybrid\mount

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%ROOT%\Tool-AutoSetup.ps1" -MountDir "%ROOT%\Hybrid\mount" -ToolRoot "%ROOT%"

echo [+] Injecting Hybrid drivers...
dism /Image:%ROOT%\Hybrid\mount /Add-Driver /Driver:%ROOT%\Hybrid\drivers\ /Recurse

echo [+] Committing Hybrid build...
dism /Unmount-WIM /MountDir:%ROOT%\Hybrid\mount /Commit

copy "%ROOT%\Hybrid\base\hybrid.wim" "%WIMS%\Hybrid_Custom.wim" /Y


REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Create Combined WIM
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Creating Combined WIM...

dism /Export-Image /SourceImageFile:%WIMS%\WinPE_Custom.wim /SourceIndex:1 /DestinationImageFile:%WIMS%\Combined.wim /Compress:max
dism /Export-Image /SourceImageFile:%WIMS%\WinRE_Custom.wim /SourceIndex:1 /DestinationImageFile:%WIMS%\Combined.wim /Compress:max
dism /Export-Image /SourceImageFile:%WIMS%\Hybrid_Custom.wim /SourceIndex:1 /DestinationImageFile:%WIMS%\Combined.wim /Compress:max


REM -----------------------------------------------------------
REM Create BCD for Combined Boot Menu
REM -----------------------------------------------------------
echo [+] Creating BCD boot menu...

set BCDSTORE=%ROOT%\Combined\BCD
bcdedit /createstore %BCDSTORE%

REM --- WinPE
for /f "tokens=2 delims={}" %%i in ('bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /create /d "WinPE Deployment Environment" /application osloader') do set PEID={%%i}
bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /set %PEID% device ramdisk=[boot]\sources\Combined.wim,{ramdiskoptions}
bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /set %PEID% path \windows\system32\boot\winload.exe

REM --- WinRE
for /f "tokens=2 delims={}" %%i in ('bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /create /d "WinRE Advanced Recovery" /application osloader') do set REID={%%i}
bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /set %REID% device ramdisk=[boot]\sources\Combined.wim,{ramdiskoptions}
bcdedit /store %BCDSTORE% /set %REID% path \windows\system32\boot\winload.exe

# ================================
# PreFlight.ps1
# ================================

Write-Host "Running PreFlight checks..."

# Check CPU
Get-WmiObject Win32_Processor | Select-Object Name, NumberOfCores, NumberOfLogicalProcessors

# Check RAM
Get-WmiObject Win32_PhysicalMemory | Select-Object Manufacturer, Capacity, Speed

# Check disk status
Get-PhysicalDisk | Select-Object FriendlyName, Size, MediaType, HealthStatus

# Check network
Get-NetAdapter | Select-Object Name, Status, LinkSpeed

# Check storage drivers
dism /Get-Drivers /Online

Write-Host "PreFlight complete."
Pause

.\Add-Tools.ps1 -MountDir $mount -ToolsDir "$root\WinPE\tools"

dism /Unmount-WIM /MountDir:$mount /Commit
Copy-Item $wim "$root\Output\WIMs\WinPE_Custom.wim" -Force

# ================================
# RecoverySuite.ps1
# ================================

Clear-Host
Write-Host "====== Advanced Recovery Suite ======"

$WindowsDrive = (Get-WmiObject Win32_OperatingSystem).SystemDrive

Write-Host "Detected Windows Drive: $WindowsDrive"
Write-Host "Running DISM / SFC repair..."

dism /Image:$WindowsDrive /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth

sfc /scannow /offbootdir=$WindowsDrive /offwindir=$WindowsDrive\Windows

Write-Host "Repairs complete."
Pause

@{
    # Module Manifest for WinPE-Builder-Suite
    # Custom Windows PE/RE Builder with GUI, Tools, and Automation

    # Script module or binary module file associated with this manifest
    RootModule = 'WinPE-Builder.psm1'

    # Version number of this module
    ModuleVersion = '1.0.0'

    # Supported PSEditions
    CompatiblePSEditions = @('Desktop', 'Core')

    # ID used to uniquely identify this module
    GUID = 'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890'

    # Author of this module
    Author = 'WinPE Builder Team'

    # Company or vendor of this module
    CompanyName = 'Custom Solutions'

    # Copyright statement for this module
    Copyright = '(c) 2025. All rights reserved.'

    # Description of the functionality provided by this module
    Description = 'Comprehensive WinPE/WinRE Custom Builder Suite with GUI menus, tool integration, driver injection, WIM editing, ISO creation, and deployment automation.'

    # Minimum version of the PowerShell engine required by this module
    PowerShellVersion = '5.1'

    # Minimum version of the CLR required by this module
    CLRVersion = '4.0'

    # Processor architecture required by this module
    ProcessorArchitecture = 'Amd64'

    # Modules that must be imported into the global environment prior to importing this module
    RequiredModules = @()

    # Assemblies that must be loaded prior to importing this module
    RequiredAssemblies = @(
        'PresentationFramework',
        'PresentationCore',
        'WindowsBase'
    )

    # Script files (.ps1) that are run in the caller's environment prior to importing this module
    ScriptsToProcess = @()

    # Type files (.ps1xml) to be loaded when importing this module
    TypesToProcess = @()

    FormatsToProcess = @()

    NestedModules = @(

arguments.content:
#Requires -Version 5.1
#Requires -RunAsAdministrator

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    WinPE-Builder-Suite Main Module Loader
.DESCRIPTION
    Comprehensive WinPE/WinRE Custom Builder Suite with GUI menus, tool integration,
    driver injection, WIM editing, ISO creation, and deployment automation.
.NOTES
    Version: 1.0.0
    Author: WinPE Builder Team
    Requires: Windows ADK, Administrator privileges
#>

using namespace System.Management.Automation
using namespace System.Collections.Generic
using namespace System.IO

#region Module Variables
$script:ModuleRoot = $PSScriptRoot
$script:ConfigPath = Join-Path -Path $script:ModuleRoot -ChildPath 'Config'
$script:OutputPath = Join-Path -Path $script:ModuleRoot -ChildPath 'Output'
$script:LogPath = Join-Path -Path $script:OutputPath -ChildPath 'Logs'
$script:ResourcePath = Join-Path -Path $script:ModuleRoot -ChildPath 'Resources'
$script:CurrentBuildPath = $null
$script:MountedImages = [Dictionary[string, object]]::new()
$script:Configuration = $null
$script:IsInitialized = $false
#endregion

#region Module Initialization
function Initialize-Module {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Initializes the WinPE Builder module.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Loads configuration, validates environment, and prepares module for use.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param()

    if ($script:IsInitialized) {
        return
    }

    # Ensure output directories exist
    $directories = @($script:OutputPath, $script:LogPath)
    foreach ($dir in $directories) {
        if (-not (Test-Path -Path $dir -PathType Container)) {
            $null = New-Item -Path $dir -ItemType Directory -Force
        }
    }

    # Load configuration
    $settingsPath = Join-Path -Path $script:ConfigPath -ChildPath 'Settings.json'
    if (Test-Path -Path $settingsPath -PathType Leaf) {
        $script:Configuration = Get-Content -Path $settingsPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    }

    $script:IsInitialized = $true
    Write-Verbose -Message 'WinPE Builder module initialized successfully.'
}
#endregion

#region Helper Functions
function Get-ModuleRoot {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the module root path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()
    return $script:ModuleRoot
}

function Get-ConfigPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the configuration directory path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()
    return $script:ConfigPath
}

function Get-OutputPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the output directory path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()
    return $script:OutputPath
}

function Get-LogPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the log directory path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()
    return $script:LogPath
}

function Get-CurrentBuildPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the current build directory path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()
    return $script:CurrentBuildPath
}

function Set-CurrentBuildPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Sets the current build directory path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path
    )
    $script:CurrentBuildPath = $Path
}

function Get-MountedImages {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the dictionary of currently mounted images.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([Dictionary[string, object]])]
    param()
    return $script:MountedImages
}

function Add-MountedImage {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Adds a mounted image to the tracking dictionary.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$MountPath,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [object]$ImageInfo
    )
    $script:MountedImages[$MountPath] = $ImageInfo
}

function Remove-MountedImage {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Removes a mounted image from the tracking dictionary.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$MountPath
    )
    if ($script:MountedImages.ContainsKey($MountPath)) {
        $null = $script:MountedImages.Remove($MountPath)
    }
}

function Test-IsAdministrator {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Tests if the current session is running as Administrator.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([bool])]
    param()

    $currentPrincipal = [Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]::new(
        [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
    )
    return $currentPrincipal.IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
}

function Assert-Administrator {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Throws an exception if not running as Administrator.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param()

    if (-not (Test-IsAdministrator)) {
        throw 'This operation requires Administrator privileges. Please run PowerShell as Administrator.'
    }
}

function Get-WindowsADKPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the Windows ADK installation path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkPaths = @(
        "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Windows Kits\10\Assessment and Deployment Kit",
        "$env:ProgramFiles\Windows Kits\10\Assessment and Deployment Kit"
    )

    foreach ($path in $adkPaths) {
        if (Test-Path -Path $path -PathType Container) {
            return $path
        }
    }

    return $null
}

function Get-WinPEToolsPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the WinPE tools path from ADK.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkPath = Get-WindowsADKPath
    if ($null -eq $adkPath) {
        return $null
    }

    $winpePath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Windows Preinstallation Environment'
    if (Test-Path -Path $winpePath -PathType Container) {
        return $winpePath
    }

    return $null
}

function Get-DeploymentToolsPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the Deployment Tools path from ADK.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkPath = Get-WindowsADKPath
    if ($null -eq $adkPath) {
        return $null
    }

    $deployPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Deployment Tools'
    if (Test-Path -Path $deployPath -PathType Container) {
        return $deployPath
    }

    return $null
}

function Get-OSCDImgPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the oscdimg.exe path for ISO creation.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $deployPath = Get-DeploymentToolsPath
    if ($null -eq $deployPath) {
        return $null
    }

    $oscdimgPath = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\Oscdimg\oscdimg.exe'
    if (Test-Path -Path $oscdimgPath -PathType Leaf) {
        return $oscdimgPath
    }

    return $null
}

function Get-DISMPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the DISM.exe path (ADK version preferred).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    # Try ADK DISM first (newer version)
    $deployPath = Get-DeploymentToolsPath
    if ($null -ne $deployPath) {
        $adkDism = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\DISM\dism.exe'
        if (Test-Path -Path $adkDism -PathType Leaf) {
            return $adkDism
        }
    }

    # Fall back to system DISM
    $systemDism = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\dism.exe"
    if (Test-Path -Path $systemDism -PathType Leaf) {
        return $systemDism
    }

    return $null
}
#endregion

#region Aliases
New-Alias -Name 'winpe-build' -Value 'Build-CustomWinPE' -Force
New-Alias -Name 'winpe-mount' -Value 'Mount-WinPEImage' -Force
New-Alias -Name 'winpe-dismount' -Value 'Dismount-WinPEImage' -Force
New-Alias -Name 'winpe-recovery' -Value 'Invoke-OneClickRecovery' -Force
New-Alias -Name 'winpe-capture' -Value 'Invoke-OneClickCapture' -Force
#endregion

#region Module Cleanup
$ExecutionContext.SessionState.Module.OnRemove = {
    # Cleanup any mounted images
    $mountedImages = Get-MountedImages
    foreach ($mountPath in $mountedImages.Keys) {
        Write-Warning -Message "Attempting to dismount image at: $mountPath"
        try {
            $null = Dismount-WindowsImage -Path $mountPath -Discard -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
        catch {
            Write-Warning -Message "Failed to dismount image at ${mountPath}: $($_.Exception.Message)"
        }
    }
}
#endregion

# Initialize module on load
Initialize-Module

    "adkRoot": "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\\Windows Kits\\10\\Assessment and Deployment Kit",
    "winPERoot": "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\\Windows Kits\\10\\Assessment and Deployment Kit\\Windows Preinstallation Environment",
    "deploymentTools": "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\\Windows Kits\\10\\Assessment and Deployment Kit\\Deployment Tools",
    "workingDirectory": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output",
    "toolsCache": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output\\ToolsCache",
    "driverStore": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output\\DriverStore",
    "isoOutput": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output\\ISO",
    "wimOutput": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output\\WIM",
    "logsDirectory": "${ModuleRoot}\\Output\\Logs",
    "tempDirectory": "${env:TEMP}\\WinPE-Builder"
  },
  
  "build": {
    "defaultMountPath": "${env:TEMP}\\WinPE-Builder\\Mount",
    "createTimestampedBuilds": true,
    "buildNameFormat": "WinPE_Custom_{0:yyyyMMdd_HHmmss}",
    "cleanupOnSuccess": true,
    "cleanupOnFailure": false,
    "compressionLevel": "Maximum",
    "splitWimSizeMB": 4000,
    "verifyBuildIntegrity": true
  },
  
  "wimSettings": {
    "defaultImageIndex": 1,
    "defaultImageName": "Microsoft Windows PE (amd64)",
    "scratchSpace": 512,
    "enableOptimizations": true,
    "removeDefaultApps": false,
    "enableLongPaths": true
  },
  
  "bootMedia": {
    "usbPartitionScheme": "DualPartition",
    "usbBootPartitionSizeMB": 1024,
    "usbFileSystem": {
      "bootPartition": "FAT32",
      "dataPartition": "NTFS"
    },
    "uefiOnly": true,
    "secureBoot": true,
    "isoVolumeLabel": "WinPE_Custom",
    "isoBootType": "UEFI"
  },
  
  "drivers": {
    "autoDetectHardware": true,
    "dynamicLoading": true,
    "injectCriticalOnly": false,
    "sources": {
      "exportFromSystem": true,
      "windowsUpdate": true,
      "manufacturerPacks": true,
      "customRepository": ""
    },
    "targetHardware": {
      "motherboard": "ASUS PRIME Z390-P",
      "cpu": "Intel Core i5-9600K",
      "gpu": "NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti",
      "storage": ["NVMe", "SATA", "USB"],
      "network": ["Intel I219-V", "Realtek RTL8111"]
    },
    "manufacturerPackUrls": {
      "intel": {
        "chipset": "https://downloadcenter.intel.com/download/chipset",
        "network": "https://downloadcenter.intel.com/download/network",
        "storage": "https://downloadcenter.intel.com/download/rst"
      },
      "nvidia": {
        "display": "https://www.nvidia.com/download/driverResults.aspx"
      },
      "realtek": {
        "network": "https://www.realtek.com/en/component/zoo/category/network-interface-controllers-10-100-1000m-gigabit-ethernet-pci-express-software"
      }
    }
  },
  
  "components": {
    "winPEPackages": [
      "WinPE-WMI",
      "WinPE-NetFx",
      "WinPE-Scripting",
      "WinPE-PowerShell",
      "WinPE-DismCmdlets",
      "WinPE-SecureBootCmdlets",
      "WinPE-StorageWMI",
      "WinPE-EnhancedStorage",
      "WinPE-FMAPI",
      "WinPE-SecureStartup",
      "WinPE-WDS-Tools",
      "WinPE-Dot3Svc",
      "WinPE-WiFi-Package",
      "WinPE-HTA",
      "WinPE-MDAC",
      "WinPE-RNDIS",
      "WinPE-PPPoE",
      "WinPE-PlatformId",
      "WinPE-Rejuv",
      "WinPE-SRT",
      "WinPE-WinReCfg",
      "WinPE-FontSupport-WinRE"
    ],
    "optionalPackages": [
      "WinPE-LegacySetup",
      "WinPE-Setup",
      "WinPE-Setup-Client"
    ],
    "languagePacks": ["en-US"],
    "fonts": ["WinPE-Font-Support-JPN", "WinPE-Font-Support-KOR", "WinPE-Font-Support-ZH-CN"]
  },
  
  "shell": {
    "defaultShell": "WinXShell",
    "fallbackShell": "cmd.exe",
    "shells": {
      "WinXShell": {
        "enabled": true,
        "configFile": "WinXShell.ini",
        "startupDelay": 2
      },
      "Pecmd": {
        "enabled": true,
        "configFile": "pecmd.ini"
      },
      "PowerShellGUI": {
        "enabled": true,
        "entryPoint": "Start-WinPEShell"
      }
    },
    "startupScript": "X:\\Windows\\System32\\startnet.cmd",
    "autoLogon": true
  },
  
  "gui": {
    "theme": "Dark",
    "availableThemes": ["Light", "Dark", "HighContrast", "Custom"],
    "accentColor": "#0078D4",
    "wallpaper": "Resources\\Wallpapers\\default.jpg",
    "customWallpaper": "",
    "showTaskbar": true,
    "taskbarPinnedTools": [
      "Explorer++",
      "Notepad++",
      "PowerShell",
      "Everything Search",
      "7-Zip"
    ],
    "menuLayout": {
      "style": "CategoryTabs",
      "showSearch": true,
      "showFavorites": true,
      "categories": [
        "Disk & Imaging",
        "File Management",
        "Backup & Sync",
        "Networking",
        "Hardware & Diagnostics",
        "Security & Malware",
        "Editing & Repair",
        "Dev Tools",
        "Deployment",
        "Recovery"
      ]
    },
    "resolution": {
      "width": 1920,
      "height": 1080,
      "colorDepth": 32
    }
  },
  
  "tools": {
    "downloadMethod": "Auto",
    "fallbackToManual": true,
    "useChocolatey": true,
    "useWinget": true,
    "alwaysDownloadLatest": true,
    "verifyChecksums": true,
    "retryCount": 3,
    "retryDelaySeconds": 5,
    "parallelDownloads": 4,
    "toolsDefinitionFile": "Tools.json",
    "extractionPath": "X:\\Tools",
    "registryIntegration": true
  },
  
  "recovery": {
    "oneClickRepairSequence": [
      "CheckDisk",
      "SFC",
      "DISM-CheckHealth",
      "DISM-ScanHealth",
      "DISM-RestoreHealth",
      "BCDRepair",
      "StartupRepair",
      "RegistryBackupRestore"
    ],
    "createRestorePoint": true,
    "backupRegistry": true,
    "offlineWindowsPath": "C:\\Windows",
    "offlineBootPath": "C:\\",
    "bitLockerRecoveryMethods": ["TPM", "RecoveryKey", "Password", "ADBackup"]
  },
  
  "capture": {
    "defaultFormat": "WIM",
    "supportedFormats": ["WIM", "ESD", "FFU", "SplitWIM"],
    "compressionType": "Maximum",
    "verifyCapture": true,
    "splitSizeMB": 4000,
    "destinations": {
      "local": true,
      "networkShare": true,
      "defaultLocalPath": "D:\\Images",
      "defaultNetworkPath": ""
    },
    "sysprep": {
      "auditMode": true,
      "removeDrivers": true,
      "generalizeMode": "OOBE",
      "shutdownAction": "Shutdown",
      "answerFile": "Templates\\Unattend-OOBE.xml",
      "preScripts": [],
      "postScripts": []
    }
  },
  
  "network": {
    "enableWiFi": true,
    "enableNetworkShares": true,
    "credentialVault": {
      "enabled": true,
      "encryptCredentials": true,
      "vaultFile": "Config\\CredentialVault.xml"
    },
    "defaultShares": [],
    "proxySettings": {
      "enabled": false,
      "server": "",
      "port": 8080,
      "bypassList": ["localhost", "127.0.0.1"]
    },
    "wakeOnLan": {
      "enabled": true,
      "defaultPort": 9,
      "broadcastAddress": "255.255.255.255"
    }
  },
  
  "security": {
    "secureWipe": {
      "defaultMethod": "DoD5220",
      "availableMethods": ["Quick", "SinglePass", "DoD5220", "DoD5220-7Pass", "Gutmann"],
      "verifyWipe": true
    },
    "defenderOffline": {
      "enabled": true,
      "updateDefinitions": true,
      "quickScanFirst": true
    },
    "tpmManagement": {
      "enabled": true,
      "allowClear": false,
      "requireConfirmation": true
    }
  },
  
  "devEnvironment": {
    "enabled": true,
    "components": {
      "powerShell7": true,
      "vsCodePortable": true,
      "gitPortable": true,
      "pythonPortable": true,
      "curlWget": true,
      "busyBox": true,
      "autoHotKey": false
    },
    "aiTools": {
      "cursor": false,
      "claudeCode": false,
      "chatGPT": false,
      "codex": false,
      "windsurf": false,
      "zed": false,
      "antiGravity": false
    },
    "mdtIntegration": {
      "enabled": true,
      "standalone": true,
      "mdtSharePath": "",
      "deploymentShareName": "DeploymentShare$"
    }
  },
  
  "logging": {
    "enabled": true,
    "level": "Information",
    "levels": ["Verbose", "Debug", "Information", "Warning", "Error", "Critical"],
    "consoleOutput": true,
    "fileOutput": true,
    "cmTraceFormat": true,
    "maxLogSizeMB": 50,
    "maxLogFiles": 10,
    "logFileName": "WinPE-Builder_{0:yyyyMMdd}.log",
    "includeTimestamp": true,
    "includeCallStack": false
  },
  
  "testing": {
    "environments": ["HyperV", "VMware", "VirtualBox", "Physical"],
    "defaultEnvironment": "HyperV",
    "autoCreateTestVM": true,
    "vmSettings": {
      "memoryMB": 4096,
      "processors": 2,
      "diskSizeGB": 60,
      "generation": 2
    }
  },
  
  "errorHandling": {
    "stopOnFirstError": false,
    "retryOnDownloadFailure": true,
    "maxRetries": 3,
    "retryDelayMs": 5000,
    "createErrorReport": true,
    "notifyOnCompletion": false
  },
  
  "updates": {
    "checkForUpdates": true,
    "autoUpdateTools": true,
    "updateCheckIntervalDays": 7,
    "lastUpdateCheck": null
  }
}

#Requires -Version 5.1

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    WinPE Builder Suite - Core Logging Module
.DESCRIPTION
    Provides comprehensive logging functionality with console output,
    file logging, and CMTrace-compatible log format support.
.NOTES
    Module: WinPE.Core.Logging
    Version: 1.0.0
#>

using namespace System.IO

#region Module Variables
$script:LogFilePath = $null
$script:LogLevel = 'Information'
$script:ConsoleOutput = $true
$script:FileOutput = $true
$script:CMTraceFormat = $true
$script:LogInitialized = $false

# Log level hierarchy
$script:LogLevels = @{
    'Verbose'     = 0
    'Debug'       = 1
    'Information' = 2
    'Warning'     = 3
    'Error'       = 4
    'Critical'    = 5
}
#endregion

#region Private Functions
function Get-CMTraceLogEntry {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Formats a log entry in CMTrace-compatible format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [ValidateSet('Verbose', 'Debug', 'Information', 'Warning', 'Error', 'Critical')]
        [string]$Level,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder',

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Context = '',

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$Thread = [System.Threading.Thread]::CurrentThread.ManagedThreadId,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$File = ''
    )

    $severityMap = @{
        'Verbose'     = 1
        'Debug'       = 1
        'Information' = 1
        'Warning'     = 2
        'Error'       = 3
        'Critical'    = 3
    }

    $severity = $severityMap[$Level]
    $time = Get-Date -Format 'HH:mm:ss.fff'
    $date = Get-Date -Format 'MM-dd-yyyy'
    $timeZoneBias = (Get-Date).ToString('zzz').Replace(':', '')

    $logEntry = "<![LOG[$Message]LOG]!>" +
        "<time=`"$time$timeZoneBias`" " +
        "date=`"$date`" " +
        "component=`"$Component`" " +
        "context=`"$Context`" " +
        "type=`"$severity`" " +
        "thread=`"$Thread`" " +
        "file=`"$File`">"

    return $logEntry
}

function Get-StandardLogEntry {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Formats a log entry in standard format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Level,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    $timestamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff'
    $levelPadded = $Level.PadRight(11)

    return "[$timestamp] [$levelPadded] [$Component] $Message"
}

function Write-ConsoleMessage {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a colored message to the console.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Level
    )

    $colorMap = @{
        'Verbose'     = 'DarkGray'
        'Debug'       = 'Gray'
        'Information' = 'White'
        'Warning'     = 'Yellow'
        'Error'       = 'Red'
        'Critical'    = 'Magenta'
    }

    $color = $colorMap[$Level]
    $prefix = switch ($Level) {
        'Verbose'     { '[V]' }
        'Debug'       { '[D]' }
        'Information' { '[I]' }
        'Warning'     { '[W]' }
        'Error'       { '[E]' }
        'Critical'    { '[!]' }
    }

    $timestamp = Get-Date -Format 'HH:mm:ss'
    Write-Host "[$timestamp] $prefix " -NoNewline -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    Write-Host $Message -ForegroundColor $color
}
#endregion

#region Public Functions
function Initialize-WinPELogging {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Initializes the logging system for WinPE Builder.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Sets up log file path, logging level, and output options.
    .PARAMETER LogPath
        Directory where log files will be created.
    .PARAMETER LogLevel
        Minimum logging level (Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical).
    .PARAMETER ConsoleOutput
        Enable console output.
    .PARAMETER FileOutput
        Enable file output.
    .PARAMETER CMTraceFormat
        Use CMTrace-compatible log format.
    .EXAMPLE
        Initialize-WinPELogging -LogPath 'C:\Logs' -LogLevel 'Information'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter()]
        [string]$LogPath,

        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('Verbose', 'Debug', 'Information', 'Warning', 'Error', 'Critical')]
        [string]$LogLevel = 'Information',

        [Parameter()]
        [bool]$ConsoleOutput = $true,

        [Parameter()]
        [bool]$FileOutput = $true,

        [Parameter()]
        [bool]$CMTraceFormat = $true
    )

    $script:LogLevel = $LogLevel
    $script:ConsoleOutput = $ConsoleOutput
    $script:FileOutput = $FileOutput
    $script:CMTraceFormat = $CMTraceFormat

    if ($FileOutput -and $LogPath) {
        if (-not (Test-Path -Path $LogPath -PathType Container)) {
            $null = New-Item -Path $LogPath -ItemType Directory -Force
        }

        $logFileName = "WinPE-Builder_$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd').log"
        $script:LogFilePath = Join-Path -Path $LogPath -ChildPath $logFileName
    }

    $script:LogInitialized = $true

    Write-WinPELog -Message 'Logging system initialized' -Level 'Information' -Component 'Logging'
    Write-WinPELog -Message "Log Level: $LogLevel" -Level 'Debug' -Component 'Logging'
    Write-WinPELog -Message "Log File: $($script:LogFilePath)" -Level 'Debug' -Component 'Logging'
}

function Write-WinPELog {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a log entry to console and/or file.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Primary logging function for the WinPE Builder suite.
        Supports multiple log levels and CMTrace format.
    .PARAMETER Message
        The log message to write.
    .PARAMETER Level
        Log level (Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical).
    .PARAMETER Component
        The component generating the log entry.
    .PARAMETER Exception
        Optional exception object to include in the log.
    .EXAMPLE
        Write-WinPELog -Message 'Starting build process' -Level 'Information'
    .EXAMPLE
        Write-WinPELog -Message 'Failed to mount image' -Level 'Error' -Exception $_.Exception
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter(Position = 1)]
        [ValidateSet('Verbose', 'Debug', 'Information', 'Warning', 'Error', 'Critical')]
        [string]$Level = 'Information',

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder',

        [Parameter()]
        [System.Exception]$Exception
    )

    # Check if log level meets threshold
    $currentLevelValue = $script:LogLevels[$Level]
    $thresholdValue = $script:LogLevels[$script:LogLevel]

    if ($currentLevelValue -lt $thresholdValue) {
        return
    }

    # Append exception details if provided
    if ($null -ne $Exception) {
        $Message = "$Message`nException: $($Exception.GetType().FullName)`nMessage: $($Exception.Message)"
        if ($Exception.InnerException) {
            $Message += "`nInner Exception: $($Exception.InnerException.Message)"
        }
    }

    # Console output
    if ($script:ConsoleOutput) {
        Write-ConsoleMessage -Message $Message -Level $Level
    }

    # File output
    if ($script:FileOutput -and $null -ne $script:LogFilePath) {
        try {
            $logEntry = if ($script:CMTraceFormat) {
                Get-CMTraceLogEntry -Message $Message -Level $Level -Component $Component
            }
            else {
                Get-StandardLogEntry -Message $Message -Level $Level -Component $Component
            }

            $logEntry | Out-File -FilePath $script:LogFilePath -Append -Encoding UTF8
        }
        catch {
            Write-Warning -Message "Failed to write to log file: $($_.Exception.Message)"
        }
    }
}

function Write-WinPEVerbose {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a verbose log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Verbose' -Component $Component
}

function Write-WinPEDebug {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a debug log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Debug' -Component $Component
}

function Write-WinPEInfo {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes an information log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Information' -Component $Component
}

function Write-WinPEWarning {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a warning log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Warning' -Component $Component
}

function Write-WinPEError {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes an error log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder',

        [Parameter()]
        [System.Exception]$Exception
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Error' -Component $Component -Exception $Exception
}

function Write-WinPECritical {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Writes a critical log entry.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)]
        [string]$Message,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder',

        [Parameter()]
        [System.Exception]$Exception
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message $Message -Level 'Critical' -Component $Component -Exception $Exception
}

function Get-WinPELogPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the current log file path.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    return $script:LogFilePath
}

function Set-WinPELogLevel {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Sets the current logging level.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [ValidateSet('Verbose', 'Debug', 'Information', 'Warning', 'Error', 'Critical')]
        [string]$Level
    )

    $script:LogLevel = $Level
    Write-WinPELog -Message "Log level changed to: $Level" -Level 'Information' -Component 'Logging'
}

function Start-WinPELogSection {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Starts a new log section with a header.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$SectionName
    )

    $separator = '=' * 80
    Write-WinPELog -Message $separator -Level 'Information'
    Write-WinPELog -Message "SECTION: $SectionName" -Level 'Information'
    Write-WinPELog -Message $separator -Level 'Information'
}

function Stop-WinPELogSection {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Ends a log section with a footer.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$SectionName,

        [Parameter()]
        [bool]$Success = $true
    )

    $status = if ($Success) { 'COMPLETED' } else { 'FAILED' }
    $separator = '-' * 80
    Write-WinPELog -Message $separator -Level 'Information'
    Write-WinPELog -Message "END SECTION: $SectionName - $status" -Level 'Information'
    Write-WinPELog -Message $separator -Level 'Information'
}

function Invoke-WinPELoggedAction {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Executes a script block with automatic logging.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Wraps a script block with try/catch and logging.
    .PARAMETER ActionName
        Name of the action for logging.
    .PARAMETER ScriptBlock
        The script block to execute.
    .PARAMETER Component
        Component name for logging.
    .EXAMPLE
        Invoke-WinPELoggedAction -ActionName 'Mount Image' -ScriptBlock { Mount-WindowsImage ... }
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$ActionName,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [scriptblock]$ScriptBlock,

        [Parameter()]
        [string]$Component = 'WinPE-Builder'
    )

    Write-WinPELog -Message "Starting: $ActionName" -Level 'Information' -Component $Component
    $stopwatch = [System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()

    try {
        $result = & $ScriptBlock
        $stopwatch.Stop()
        Write-WinPELog -Message "Completed: $ActionName (Duration: $($stopwatch.Elapsed.TotalSeconds.ToString('F2'))s)" -Level 'Information' -Component $Component
        return $result
    }
    catch {
        $stopwatch.Stop()
        Write-WinPELog -Message "Failed: $ActionName (Duration: $($stopwatch.Elapsed.TotalSeconds.ToString('F2'))s)" -Level 'Error' -Component $Component -Exception $_.Exception
        throw
    }
}
#endregion

#region Module Exports
Export-ModuleMember -Function @(
    'Initialize-WinPELogging',
    'Write-WinPELog',
    'Write-WinPEVerbose',
    'Write-WinPEDebug',
    'Write-WinPEInfo',
    'Write-WinPEWarning',
    'Write-WinPEError',
    'Write-WinPECritical',
    'Get-WinPELogPath',
    'Set-WinPELogLevel',
    'Start-WinPELogSection',
    'Stop-WinPELogSection',
    'Invoke-WinPELoggedAction'
)
#endregion

#Requires -Version 5.1
#Requires -RunAsAdministrator

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    WinPE Builder Suite - Core Environment Module
.DESCRIPTION
    Manages the build environment including ADK detection,
    path setup, and prerequisite validation.
.NOTES
    Module: WinPE.Core.Environment
    Version: 1.0.0
#>

using namespace System.Collections.Generic

#region Module Variables
$script:EnvironmentInitialized = $false
$script:ADKInfo = $null
$script:BuildEnvironment = $null
#endregion

#region Private Functions
function Get-RegistryValue {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Safely retrieves a registry value.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Name,

        [Parameter()]
        $DefaultValue = $null
    )

    try {
        if (Test-Path -Path $Path) {
            $value = Get-ItemProperty -Path $Path -Name $Name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
            if ($null -ne $value) {
                return $value.$Name
            }
        }
    }
    catch {
        # Ignore errors, return default
    }

    return $DefaultValue
}
#endregion

#region Public Functions
function Initialize-WinPEEnvironment {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Initializes the WinPE build environment.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Detects Windows ADK, validates prerequisites, and sets up paths.
    .PARAMETER OutputPath
        Root path for build output.
    .PARAMETER Force
        Force re-initialization even if already initialized.
    .EXAMPLE
        Initialize-WinPEEnvironment -OutputPath 'C:\WinPE-Builder\Output'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter()]
        [string]$OutputPath,

        [Parameter()]
        [switch]$Force
    )

    if ($script:EnvironmentInitialized -and -not $Force) {
        return $script:BuildEnvironment
    }

    Write-Verbose -Message 'Initializing WinPE build environment...'

    # Detect Windows ADK
    $script:ADKInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    if ($null -eq $script:ADKInfo) {
        throw 'Windows ADK not detected. Please install Windows ADK with WinPE add-on.'
    }

    # Create build environment object
    $script:BuildEnvironment = [PSCustomObject]@{
        ADK                = $script:ADKInfo
        Paths              = [PSCustomObject]@{
            Output         = $OutputPath
            Mount          = Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath 'WinPE-Builder\Mount'
            ToolsCache     = Join-Path -Path $OutputPath -ChildPath 'ToolsCache'
            DriverStore    = Join-Path -Path $OutputPath -ChildPath 'DriverStore'
            ISO            = Join-Path -Path $OutputPath -ChildPath 'ISO'
            WIM            = Join-Path -Path $OutputPath -ChildPath 'WIM'
            Logs           = Join-Path -Path $OutputPath -ChildPath 'Logs'
            Temp           = Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath 'WinPE-Builder'
        }
        SystemInfo         = Get-SystemEnvironmentInfo
        Prerequisites      = @{}
        Initialized        = $false
        InitializedAt      = $null
    }

    # Create required directories
    $directories = @(
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.Output,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.Mount,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.ToolsCache,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.DriverStore,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.ISO,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.WIM,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.Logs,
        $script:BuildEnvironment.Paths.Temp
    )

    foreach ($dir in $directories) {
        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($dir) -and -not (Test-Path -Path $dir -PathType Container)) {
            $null = New-Item -Path $dir -ItemType Directory -Force
        }
    }

    # Check prerequisites
    $script:BuildEnvironment.Prerequisites = Test-WinPEPrerequisitesInternal

    $script:BuildEnvironment.Initialized = $true
    $script:BuildEnvironment.InitializedAt = Get-Date
    $script:EnvironmentInitialized = $true

    return $script:BuildEnvironment
}

function Get-WindowsADKInfo {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Detects and returns Windows ADK installation information.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Searches for Windows ADK installation and returns paths and version info.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WindowsADKInfo
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param()

    # Registry paths for ADK
    $adkRegPaths = @(
        'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows Kits\Installed Roots',
        'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Kits\Installed Roots'
    )

    $kitsRoot = $null
    foreach ($regPath in $adkRegPaths) {
        $kitsRoot = Get-RegistryValue -Path $regPath -Name 'KitsRoot10'
        if ($kitsRoot) {
            break
        }
    }

    if (-not $kitsRoot) {
        # Try default paths
        $defaultPaths = @(
            "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Windows Kits\10",
            "$env:ProgramFiles\Windows Kits\10"
        )

        foreach ($path in $defaultPaths) {
            if (Test-Path -Path $path -PathType Container) {
                $kitsRoot = $path
                break
            }
        }
    }

    if (-not $kitsRoot -or -not (Test-Path -Path $kitsRoot -PathType Container)) {
        return $null
    }

    $adkPath = Join-Path -Path $kitsRoot -ChildPath 'Assessment and Deployment Kit'
    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $adkPath -PathType Container)) {
        return $null
    }

    $winpePath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Windows Preinstallation Environment'
    $deployPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Deployment Tools'

    # Get version
    $version = $null
    $dismPath = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\DISM\dism.exe'
    if (Test-Path -Path $dismPath -PathType Leaf) {
        $version = (Get-Item -Path $dismPath).VersionInfo.FileVersion
    }

    # Get available architectures
    $architectures = @()
    foreach ($arch in @('amd64', 'x86', 'arm64')) {
        $archPath = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath $arch
        if (Test-Path -Path $archPath -PathType Container) {
            $architectures += $arch
        }
    }

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        KitsRoot           = $kitsRoot
        ADKPath            = $adkPath
        WinPEPath          = $winpePath
        DeploymentTools    = $deployPath
        Version            = $version
        Architectures      = $architectures
        Tools              = [PSCustomObject]@{
            DISM           = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\DISM\dism.exe'
            OSCDImg        = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\Oscdimg\oscdimg.exe'
            ImageX         = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\DISM\imagex.exe'
            BCDBoot        = Join-Path -Path $deployPath -ChildPath 'amd64\BCDBoot\bcdboot.exe'
            MakeWinPEMedia = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'MakeWinPEMedia.cmd'
            CopyPE         = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'copype.cmd'
        }
        Packages           = [PSCustomObject]@{
            amd64          = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'amd64\WinPE_OCs'
            x86            = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'x86\WinPE_OCs'
            arm64          = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'arm64\WinPE_OCs'
        }
        BaseImages         = [PSCustomObject]@{
            amd64          = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'amd64\en-us\winpe.wim'
            x86            = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'x86\en-us\winpe.wim'
            arm64          = Join-Path -Path $winpePath -ChildPath 'arm64\en-us\winpe.wim'
        }
    }
}

function Get-SystemEnvironmentInfo {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Retrieves system environment information.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Collects OS version, architecture, memory, and other system details.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-SystemEnvironmentInfo
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param()

    $os = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_OperatingSystem
    $cs = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_ComputerSystem
    $proc = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Processor | Select-Object -First 1

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        ComputerName       = $env:COMPUTERNAME
        OSName             = $os.Caption
        OSVersion          = $os.Version
        OSBuild            = $os.BuildNumber
        Architecture       = $os.OSArchitecture
        ProcessorName      = $proc.Name
        ProcessorCores     = $proc.NumberOfCores
        TotalMemoryGB      = [Math]::Round($cs.TotalPhysicalMemory / 1GB, 2)
        FreeMemoryGB       = [Math]::Round($os.FreePhysicalMemory / 1MB / 1024, 2)
        SystemDrive        = $env:SystemDrive
        TempPath           = $env:TEMP
        PowerShellVersion  = $PSVersionTable.PSVersion.ToString()
        IsAdministrator    = ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal][Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
        Is64BitOS          = [Environment]::Is64BitOperatingSystem
        Is64BitProcess     = [Environment]::Is64BitProcess
    }
}

function Test-WinPEPrerequisites {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Tests all prerequisites for WinPE building.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Validates ADK installation, permissions, disk space, and other requirements.
    .PARAMETER Detailed
        Return detailed information about each check.
    .EXAMPLE
        Test-WinPEPrerequisites -Detailed
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter()]
        [switch]$Detailed
    )

    $results = Test-WinPEPrerequisitesInternal

    if ($Detailed) {
        return $results
    }

    return $results.AllPassed
}

function Test-WinPEPrerequisitesInternal {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Internal function to test prerequisites.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param()

    $checks = [List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
    $allPassed = $true

    # Check: Administrator privileges
    $isAdmin = ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal][Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'Administrator'
        Description = 'Running with administrator privileges'
        Passed      = $isAdmin
        Details     = if ($isAdmin) { 'Running as Administrator' } else { 'Administrator privileges required' }
    })
    if (-not $isAdmin) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: Windows ADK
    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    $adkInstalled = $null -ne $adkInfo
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'WindowsADK'
        Description = 'Windows ADK installed'
        Passed      = $adkInstalled
        Details     = if ($adkInstalled) { "Version: $($adkInfo.Version)" } else { 'Windows ADK not found' }
    })
    if (-not $adkInstalled) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: WinPE Add-on
    $winpeInstalled = $adkInstalled -and (Test-Path -Path $adkInfo.WinPEPath -PathType Container)
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'WinPEAddon'
        Description = 'Windows PE add-on installed'
        Passed      = $winpeInstalled
        Details     = if ($winpeInstalled) { "Path: $($adkInfo.WinPEPath)" } else { 'WinPE add-on not found' }
    })
    if (-not $winpeInstalled) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: Base WinPE image
    $baseImageExists = $adkInstalled -and (Test-Path -Path $adkInfo.BaseImages.amd64 -PathType Leaf)
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'BaseWinPEImage'
        Description = 'Base WinPE.wim exists'
        Passed      = $baseImageExists
        Details     = if ($baseImageExists) { "Path: $($adkInfo.BaseImages.amd64)" } else { 'Base WinPE.wim not found' }
    })
    if (-not $baseImageExists) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: DISM available
    $dismPath = if ($adkInstalled) { $adkInfo.Tools.DISM } else { "$env:SystemRoot\System32\dism.exe" }
    $dismExists = Test-Path -Path $dismPath -PathType Leaf
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'DISM'
        Description = 'DISM.exe available'
        Passed      = $dismExists
        Details     = if ($dismExists) { "Path: $dismPath" } else { 'DISM.exe not found' }
    })
    if (-not $dismExists) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: OSCDImg for ISO creation
    $oscdimgPath = if ($adkInstalled) { $adkInfo.Tools.OSCDImg } else { $null }
    $oscdimgExists = $oscdimgPath -and (Test-Path -Path $oscdimgPath -PathType Leaf)
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'OSCDImg'
        Description = 'OSCDImg.exe for ISO creation'
        Passed      = $oscdimgExists
        Details     = if ($oscdimgExists) { "Path: $oscdimgPath" } else { 'OSCDImg.exe not found' }
    })
    if (-not $oscdimgExists) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: Available disk space (minimum 20GB)
    $systemDrive = $env:SystemDrive
    $drive = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter "DeviceID='$systemDrive'"
    $freeSpaceGB = [Math]::Round($drive.FreeSpace / 1GB, 2)
    $hasSpace = $freeSpaceGB -ge 20
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'DiskSpace'
        Description = 'Minimum 20GB free disk space'
        Passed      = $hasSpace
        Details     = "Free space on $systemDrive`: $freeSpaceGB GB"
    })
    if (-not $hasSpace) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: PowerShell version
    $psVersion = $PSVersionTable.PSVersion
    $psVersionOk = $psVersion.Major -ge 5
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'PowerShellVersion'
        Description = 'PowerShell 5.1 or higher'
        Passed      = $psVersionOk
        Details     = "Version: $psVersion"
    })
    if (-not $psVersionOk) { $allPassed = $false }

    # Check: Hyper-V (optional, for testing)
    $hyperv = Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -Online -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    $hypervEnabled = $hyperv -and $hyperv.State -eq 'Enabled'
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'HyperV'
        Description = 'Hyper-V enabled (optional, for testing)'
        Passed      = $hypervEnabled
        Details     = if ($hypervEnabled) { 'Hyper-V is enabled' } else { 'Hyper-V not enabled (optional)' }
        Optional    = $true
    })

    # Check: Internet connectivity (optional, for downloads)
    $internetOk = $false
    try {
        $null = Test-Connection -ComputerName 'www.microsoft.com' -Count 1 -Quiet -ErrorAction Stop
        $internetOk = $true
    }
    catch {
        $internetOk = $false
    }
    $checks.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
        Name        = 'InternetConnectivity'
        Description = 'Internet connectivity (optional, for downloads)'
        Passed      = $internetOk
        Details     = if ($internetOk) { 'Internet is accessible' } else { 'No internet connection (optional)' }
        Optional    = $true
    })

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        AllPassed     = $allPassed
        Checks        = $checks.ToArray()
        CheckedAt     = Get-Date
        RequiredCount = ($checks | Where-Object { -not $_.Optional }).Count
        PassedCount   = ($checks | Where-Object { $_.Passed -and -not $_.Optional }).Count
        FailedCount   = ($checks | Where-Object { -not $_.Passed -and -not $_.Optional }).Count
    }
}

function Get-WinPEEnvironment {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the current build environment.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEEnvironment
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param()

    if (-not $script:EnvironmentInitialized) {
        Write-Warning -Message 'Environment not initialized. Call Initialize-WinPEEnvironment first.'
        return $null
    }

    return $script:BuildEnvironment
}

function Get-WinPEADKPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the Windows ADK installation path.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEADKPath
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    return $adkInfo.ADKPath
}

function Get-WinPEBasePath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the WinPE base path from ADK.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEBasePath
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    return $adkInfo.WinPEPath
}

function Get-WinPEPackagesPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the WinPE optional components path.
    .PARAMETER Architecture
        Target architecture (amd64, x86, arm64).
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEPackagesPath -Architecture 'amd64'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('amd64', 'x86', 'arm64')]
        [string]$Architecture = 'amd64'
    )

    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    return $adkInfo.Packages.$Architecture
}

function Get-WinPEBaseImage {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the path to the base WinPE.wim.
    .PARAMETER Architecture
        Target architecture (amd64, x86, arm64).
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEBaseImage -Architecture 'amd64'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param(
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('amd64', 'x86', 'arm64')]
        [string]$Architecture = 'amd64'
    )

    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    return $adkInfo.BaseImages.$Architecture
}

function Set-WinPEEnvironmentVariable {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Sets an environment variable for the build session.
    .PARAMETER Name
        Variable name.
    .PARAMETER Value
        Variable value.
    .PARAMETER Scope
        Variable scope (Process, User, Machine).
    .EXAMPLE
        Set-WinPEEnvironmentVariable -Name 'WINPE_OUTPUT' -Value 'C:\Output'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Name,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Value,

        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('Process', 'User', 'Machine')]
        [string]$Scope = 'Process'
    )

    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($Name, $Value, $Scope)
    Write-Verbose -Message "Environment variable set: $Name = $Value (Scope: $Scope)"
}

function Get-WinPEDeploymentToolsPath {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Returns the Deployment Tools path from ADK.
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-WinPEDeploymentToolsPath
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([string])]
    param()

    $adkInfo = Get-WindowsADKInfo
    return $adkInfo.DeploymentTools
}

function Test-WinPEEnvironmentReady {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Tests if the environment is ready for building.
    .EXAMPLE
        if (Test-WinPEEnvironmentReady) { Start-Build }
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([bool])]
    param()

    if (-not $script:EnvironmentInitialized) {
        return $false
    }

    $prereqs = Test-WinPEPrerequisites
    return $prereqs
}
#endregion

#region Module Exports
Export-ModuleMember -Function @(
    'Initialize-WinPEEnvironment',
    'Get-WindowsADKInfo',
    'Get-SystemEnvironmentInfo',
    'Test-WinPEPrerequisites',
    'Get-WinPEEnvironment',
    'Get-WinPEADKPath',
    'Get-WinPEBasePath',
    'Get-WinPEPackagesPath',
    'Get-WinPEBaseImage',
    'Set-WinPEEnvironmentVariable',
    'Get-WinPEDeploymentToolsPath',
    'Test-WinPEEnvironmentReady'
)
#endregion

arguments.content:
@{
    # Module manifest for WinPE-Builder-Suite
    RootModule        = 'WinPE-Builder.psm1'
    ModuleVersion     = '1.0.0'
    GUID              = 'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890'
    Author            = 'WinPE-Builder-Suite'
    CompanyName       = 'WinPE-Builder'
    Copyright         = '(c) 2025. All rights reserved.'
    Description       = 'Comprehensive WinPE/WinRE Custom Builder Suite with GUI, Tool Integration, and Deployment Automation'
    PowerShellVersion = '5.1'
    
    # Nested modules to load
    NestedModules     = @(
        'Modules\WimManager\WimManager.psm1'
        'Modules\ToolManager\ToolManager.psm1'
        'Modules\DriverManager\DriverManager.psm1'
        'Modules\ISOBuilder\ISOBuilder.psm1'
        'Modules\GUIBuilder\GUIBuilder.psm1'
        'Modules\RecoveryTools\RecoveryTools.psm1'
        'Modules\CaptureTools\CaptureTools.psm1'
        'Modules\NetworkConfig\NetworkConfig.psm1'
        'Modules\DevEnvironment\DevEnvironment.psm1'
    )
    
    # Functions to export
    FunctionsToExport = @(
        # Main orchestrator
        'Start-WinPEBuilder'
        'New-WinPEBuild'
        'Get-WinPEBuilderConfig'
        'Set-WinPEBuilderConfig'
        'Test-WinPEBuilderPrerequisites'
        'Initialize-WinPEBuilderEnvironment'
        
        # WIM Management
        'New-WinPEWim'
        'Mount-WinPEWim'
        'Dismount-WinPEWim'
        'Add-WinPEPackage'
        'Remove-WinPEPackage'
        'Get-WinPEPackages'
        'Add-WinPEDriver'
        'Export-WinPEWim'
        'Convert-WimToEsd'
        'Convert-WimToFfu'
        'Split-WinPEWim'
        'Optimize-WinPEWim'
        
        # Tool Management
        'Get-ToolManifest'
        'Install-WinPETool'
        'Install-AllWinPETools'
        'Update-WinPETool'
        'Remove-WinPETool'
        'Test-ToolAvailability'
        'Get-ToolDownloadUrl'
        'Invoke-ToolDownload'
        
        # Driver Management
        'Get-SystemDrivers'
        'Export-SystemDrivers'
        'Get-DriverDownloadUrls'
        'Install-WinPEDrivers'
        'New-DriverCab'
        'Import-DriverCab'
        'Test-DriverCompatibility'
        
        # ISO/USB Builder
        'New-WinPEISO'
        'New-WinPEUSB'
        'New-DualPartitionUSB'
        'Test-BootMedia'
        'Get-USBDrives'
        
        # GUI Builder
        'Install-WinXShell'
        'Install-PowerShellGUI'
        'Set-WinPETheme'
        'Add-StartMenuEntry'
        'Add-TaskbarShortcut'
        'New-CategoryMenu'
        
        # Recovery Tools
        'Start-OneClickRecovery'
        'Invoke-SystemFileChecker'
        'Invoke-DISMRestoreHealth'
        'Repair-BootConfiguration'
        'Repair-StartupProblems'
        'Restore-RegistryBackup'
        'Get-BitLockerRecoveryKey'
        'Unlock-BitLockerDrive'
        'Get-VSSSnapshots'
        'Restore-VSSSnapshot'
        'Install-OfflineWindowsUpdate'
        'Get-OfflineEventLogs'
        
        # Capture Tools
        'Start-OneClickCapture'
        'Enter-AuditMode'
        'New-AnswerFile'
        'Start-Sysprep'
        'New-WindowsCapture'
        'Export-CaptureToNetwork'
        
        # Network Config
        'Initialize-WinPENetwork'
        'Connect-WiFiNetwork'
        'Mount-NetworkShare'
        'Get-CredentialVault'
        'Set-CredentialVault'
        'Send-WakeOnLan'
        'Test-NetworkSpeed'
        
        # Dev Environment
        'Install-DevEnvironment'
        'Install-PowerShell7'
        'Install-VSCodePortable'
        'Install-GitPortable'
        'Initialize-MDTWorkbench'
    )
    
    # Cmdlets to export
    CmdletsToExport   = @()
    
    # Variables to export
    VariablesToExport = @(
        'WinPEBuilderConfig'
        'WinPEBuilderRoot'
    )
    
    # Aliases to export
    AliasesToExport   = @(
        'wpeb'
        'New-WinPE'
        'Build-WinPE'
    )
    
    # Private data
    PrivateData       = @{
        PSData = @{
            Tags         = @('WinPE', 'WinRE', 'Deployment', 'Recovery', 'Windows', 'ADK')
            LicenseUri   = ''
            ProjectUri   = ''
            ReleaseNotes = 'Initial release of WinPE-Builder-Suite'
        }
    }
}
