#Requires -Version 5.1
#Requires -RunAsAdministrator
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    ISOBuilder Module - ISO and USB Creation
.DESCRIPTION
    Creates bootable ISO files and USB drives from WinPE WIM images
.NOTES
    Part of WinPE-Builder-Suite
#>

using namespace System.IO
using namespace System.Collections.Generic

#region Module Variables
$script:ModuleRoot = $PSScriptRoot
$script:OscdimgPath = $null
$script:EtfsbootPath = $null
$script:EfisysPath = $null
#endregion

#region Initialization
function Initialize-ISOBuilder {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Initializes the ISOBuilder module
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param()
    
    # Find ADK tools
    $adkPaths = @(
        'C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Assessment and Deployment Kit',
        "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Windows Kits\10\Assessment and Deployment Kit"
    )
    
    foreach ($adkPath in $adkPaths) {
        $oscdimg = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Deployment Tools\amd64\Oscdimg\oscdimg.exe'
        if (Test-Path -Path $oscdimg) {
            $script:OscdimgPath = $oscdimg
            $script:EtfsbootPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Deployment Tools\amd64\Oscdimg\etfsboot.com'
            $script:EfisysPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath 'Deployment Tools\amd64\Oscdimg\efisys.bin'
            break
        }
    }
    
    if (-not $script:OscdimgPath) {
        throw "oscdimg.exe not found. Please install Windows ADK."
    }
}
#endregion

#region ISO Creation
function New-WinPEISO {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Creates a bootable ISO from WinPE files
    .DESCRIPTION
        Creates a UEFI/BIOS bootable ISO file using oscdimg
    .PARAMETER SourcePath
        Path to WinPE media files (containing boot.wim)
    .PARAMETER WimPath
        Path to boot.wim file (alternative to SourcePath)
    .PARAMETER OutputPath
        Path for the output ISO file
    .PARAMETER BootType
        Boot type: UEFI, BIOS, or Both
    .PARAMETER VolumeLabel
        Volume label for the ISO (max 32 chars)
    .EXAMPLE
        New-WinPEISO -SourcePath 'C:\WinPE\Media' -OutputPath 'C:\Output\WinPE.iso'
    .EXAMPLE
        New-WinPEISO -WimPath 'C:\boot.wim' -OutputPath 'C:\Output\WinPE.iso' -BootType UEFI
    #>
    [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true, ParameterSetName = 'BySource')]
        [ValidateScript({ Test-Path -Path $_ -PathType Container })]
        [string]$SourcePath,
        
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true, ParameterSetName = 'ByWim')]
        [ValidateScript({ Test-Path -Path $_ -PathType Leaf })]
        [string]$WimPath,
        
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
        [string]$OutputPath,
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('UEFI', 'BIOS', 'Both')]
        [string]$BootType = 'UEFI',
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateLength(1, 32)]
        [string]$VolumeLabel = 'WINPE_CUSTOM'
    )
    
    begin {
        Initialize-ISOBuilder
    }
    
    process {
        try {
            Write-Host ''
            Write-Host '╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host '║              ISO CREATION PROCESS                             ║' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host '╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host ''
            
            # Prepare media structure if using WIM directly
            $mediaPath = $SourcePath
            $tempMedia = $false
            
            if ($WimPath) {
                Write-Host "  Preparing ISO media structure..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                $mediaPath = Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath "WinPE_Media_$(Get-Random)"
                $tempMedia = $true
                
                # Create media structure
                New-Item -Path $mediaPath -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                New-Item -Path (Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'sources') -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                New-Item -Path (Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'boot') -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                New-Item -Path (Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'EFI\Microsoft\Boot') -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                New-Item -Path (Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'EFI\Boot') -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                
                # Copy WIM
                Copy-Item -Path $WimPath -Destination (Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'sources\boot.wim') -Force
                
                # Copy boot files from ADK
                $adkPath = Split-Path -Path (Split-Path -Path $script:OscdimgPath -Parent) -Parent
                $bootFilesPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath '..\Windows Preinstallation Environment\amd64\Media'
                
                if (Test-Path -Path $bootFilesPath) {
                    Copy-Item -Path "$bootFilesPath\*" -Destination $mediaPath -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                }
                
                # Ensure boot files exist
                $bootmgrPath = Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'bootmgr'
                if (-not (Test-Path -Path $bootmgrPath)) {
                    # Copy from Windows
                    Copy-Item -Path "$env:SystemRoot\Boot\bootmgr" -Destination $bootmgrPath -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                }
            }
            
            # Verify required files
            $bootWim = Join-Path -Path $mediaPath -ChildPath 'sources\boot.wim'
            if (-not (Test-Path -Path $bootWim)) {
                throw "boot.wim not found in media path"
            }
            
            # Ensure output directory exists
            $outputDir = Split-Path -Path $OutputPath -Parent
            if (-not (Test-Path -Path $outputDir)) {
                New-Item -Path $outputDir -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
            }
            
            if ($PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess($OutputPath, 'Create ISO')) {
                Write-Host "  Creating ISO: $(Split-Path -Path $OutputPath -Leaf)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
                Write-Host "  Boot Type: $BootType" -ForegroundColor Gray
                
                # Build oscdimg arguments
                $oscdimgArgs = [List[string]]::new()
                
                switch ($BootType) {
                    'UEFI' {
                        # UEFI only
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add("-bootdata:1#pEF,e,b`"$($script:EfisysPath)`"")
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add('-u2')
                    }
                    'BIOS' {
                        # BIOS only
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add("-b`"$($script:EtfsbootPath)`"")
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add('-u2')
                    }
                    'Both' {
                        # Dual boot BIOS + UEFI
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add("-bootdata:2#p0,e,b`"$($script:EtfsbootPath)`"#pEF,e,b`"$($script:EfisysPath)`"")
                        $oscdimgArgs.Add('-u2')
                    }
                }
                
                # Common options
                $oscdimgArgs.Add('-udfver102')
                $oscdimgArgs.Add("-l$VolumeLabel")
                $oscdimgArgs.Add("-o")  # Optimize
                $oscdimgArgs.Add("-h")  # Hidden files
                $oscdimgArgs.Add("`"$mediaPath`"")
                $oscdimgArgs.Add("`"$OutputPath`"")
                
                $argsString = $oscdimgArgs -join ' '
                
                Write-Verbose "Running: oscdimg.exe $argsString"
                
                $process = Start-Process -FilePath $script:OscdimgPath -ArgumentList $argsString -Wait -PassThru -NoNewWindow -RedirectStandardOutput (Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath 'oscdimg_out.txt') -RedirectStandardError (Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath 'oscdimg_err.txt')
                
                if ($process.ExitCode -ne 0) {
                    $errorOutput = Get-Content -Path (Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath 'oscdimg_err.txt') -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                    throw "oscdimg failed with exit code $($process.ExitCode): $errorOutput"
                }
                
                # Cleanup temp media
                if ($tempMedia -and (Test-Path -Path $mediaPath)) {
                    Remove-Item -Path $mediaPath -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                }
                
                # Verify output
                if (Test-Path -Path $OutputPath) {
                    $isoInfo = Get-Item -Path $OutputPath
                    Write-Host ''
                    Write-Host "  ✓ ISO created successfully!" -ForegroundColor Green
                    Write-Host "    Path: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor White
                    Write-Host "    Size: $([math]::Round($isoInfo.Length / 1MB, 2)) MB" -ForegroundColor White
                    Write-Host ''
                    
                    return [PSCustomObject]@{
                        ISOPath     = $OutputPath
                        Size        = $isoInfo.Length
                        BootType    = $BootType
                        VolumeLabel = $VolumeLabel
                        Status      = 'Created'
                        CreatedAt   = Get-Date
                    }
                }
                else {
                    throw "ISO file was not created"
                }
            }
        }
        catch {
            Write-Error "Failed to create ISO: $_"
            return [PSCustomObject]@{
                ISOPath = $OutputPath
                Status  = 'Failed'
                Error   = $_.Exception.Message
            }
        }
    }
}
#endregion

#region USB Creation
function Get-USBDrives {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Gets available USB drives
    .DESCRIPTION
        Lists removable USB drives suitable for WinPE installation
    .EXAMPLE
        Get-USBDrives
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
    param()
    
    process {
        try {
            $usbDrives = Get-Disk | Where-Object {
                $_.BusType -eq 'USB' -and $_.OperationalStatus -eq 'Online'
            }
            
            $results = foreach ($disk in $usbDrives) {
                $partition = Get-Partition -DiskNumber $disk.Number -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.DriveLetter }
                $volume = if ($partition) { Get-Volume -DriveLetter $partition.DriveLetter[0] -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } else { $null }
                
                [PSCustomObject]@{
                    DiskNumber    = $disk.Number
                    FriendlyName  = $disk.FriendlyName
                    Size          = [math]::Round($disk.Size / 1GB, 2)
                    SizeGB        = "$([math]::Round($disk.Size / 1GB, 2)) GB"
                    DriveLetter   = if ($partition) { $partition.DriveLetter } else { 'None' }
                    FileSystem    = if ($volume) { $volume.FileSystem } else { 'Unknown' }
                    PartitionStyle = $disk.PartitionStyle
                    IsRemovable   = $disk.IsSystem -eq $false
                }
            }
            
            return $results
        }
        catch {
            Write-Error "Failed to get USB drives: $_"
            throw
        }
    }
}

function New-WinPEUSB {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Creates a bootable WinPE USB drive
    .DESCRIPTION
        Formats and prepares a USB drive with WinPE for UEFI boot
    .PARAMETER DriveLetter
        Target USB drive letter
    .PARAMETER DiskNumber
        Target USB disk number (alternative to DriveLetter)
    .PARAMETER WimPath
        Path to boot.wim
    .PARAMETER SourcePath
        Path to WinPE media files (alternative to WimPath)
    .PARAMETER PartitionScheme
        Partition scheme: Single (FAT32) or DualPartition (FAT32 + NTFS)
    .PARAMETER VolumeLabel
        Volume label for the boot partition
    .EXAMPLE
        New-WinPEUSB -DriveLetter 'E' -WimPath 'C:\boot.wim'
    .EXAMPLE
        New-WinPEUSB -DiskNumber 2 -SourcePath 'C:\WinPE\Media' -PartitionScheme DualPartition
    #>
    [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess, ConfirmImpact = 'High')]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true, ParameterSetName = 'ByLetter')]
        [ValidatePattern('^[A-Z]:?$')]
        [string]$DriveLetter,
        
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true, ParameterSetName = 'ByNumber')]
        [int]$DiskNumber,
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateScript({ Test-Path -Path $_ -PathType Leaf })]
        [string]$WimPath,
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateScript({ Test-Path -Path $_ -PathType Container })]
        [string]$SourcePath,
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateSet('Single', 'DualPartition')]
        [string]$PartitionScheme = 'Single',
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateLength(1, 11)]
        [string]$VolumeLabel = 'WINPE'
    )
    
    begin {
        if (-not $WimPath -and -not $SourcePath) {
            throw "Either WimPath or SourcePath must be specified"
        }
    }
    
    process {
        try {
            Write-Host ''
            Write-Host '╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host '║              USB CREATION PROCESS                             ║' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host '╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝' -ForegroundColor Cyan
            Write-Host ''
            
            # Get target disk
            $targetDisk = if ($DriveLetter) {
                $letter = $DriveLetter.TrimEnd(':')
                $partition = Get-Partition -DriveLetter $letter -ErrorAction Stop
                Get-Disk -Number $partition.DiskNumber
            }
            else {
                Get-Disk -Number $DiskNumber -ErrorAction Stop
            }
            
            # Verify USB drive
            if ($targetDisk.BusType -ne 'USB') {
                throw "Disk $($targetDisk.Number) is not a USB drive"
            }
            
            Write-Host "  Target: $($targetDisk.FriendlyName)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            Write-Host "  Size:   $([math]::Round($targetDisk.Size / 1GB, 2)) GB" -ForegroundColor Gray
            Write-Host ''
            Write-Host '  ⚠ WARNING: ALL DATA ON THIS DRIVE WILL BE ERASED!' -ForegroundColor Red
            Write-Host ''
            
            if ($PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess("Disk $($targetDisk.Number) - $($targetDisk.FriendlyName)", 'Format and create bootable USB')) {
                
                Write-Host "  Step 1: Cleaning disk..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                
                # Clean and initialize disk
                Clear-Disk -Number $targetDisk.Number -RemoveData -RemoveOEM -Confirm:$false
                Initialize-Disk -Number $targetDisk.Number -PartitionStyle GPT -Confirm:$false
                
                if ($PartitionScheme -eq 'DualPartition') {
                    Write-Host "  Step 2: Creating dual partition layout..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                    
                    # Create FAT32 boot partition (1GB)
                    $bootPartition = New-Partition -DiskNumber $targetDisk.Number -Size 1GB -GptType '{c12a7328-f81f-11d2-ba4b-00a0c93ec93b}'
                    Format-Volume -Partition $bootPartition -FileSystem FAT32 -NewFileSystemLabel "$VolumeLabel`_BOOT" -Confirm:$false | Out-Null
                    $bootLetter = $bootPartition | Add-PartitionAccessPath -AssignDriveLetter -PassThru | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DriveLetter
                    
                    # Create NTFS data partition (remaining space)
                    $dataPartition = New-Partition -DiskNumber $targetDisk.Number -UseMaximumSize -GptType '{ebd0a0a2-b9e5-4433-87c0-68b6b72699c7}'
                    Format-Volume -Partition $dataPartition -FileSystem NTFS -NewFileSystemLabel "$VolumeLabel`_DATA" -Confirm:$false | Out-Null
                    $dataLetter = $dataPartition | Add-PartitionAccessPath -AssignDriveLetter -PassThru | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DriveLetter
                }
                else {
                    Write-Host "  Step 2: Creating single FAT32 partition..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                    
                    # Create single FAT32 partition (max 32GB for FAT32)
                    $maxFat32Size = [math]::Min($targetDisk.Size - 1MB, 32GB)
                    $bootPartition = New-Partition -DiskNumber $targetDisk.Number -Size $maxFat32Size -GptType '{c12a7328-f81f-11d2-ba4b-00a0c93ec93b}'
                    Format-Volume -Partition $bootPartition -FileSystem FAT32 -NewFileSystemLabel $VolumeLabel -Confirm:$false | Out-Null
                    $bootLetter = $bootPartition | Add-PartitionAccessPath -AssignDriveLetter -PassThru | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DriveLetter
                    $dataLetter = $null
                }
                
                Write-Host "  Step 3: Copying boot files..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                
                $bootDrive = "${bootLetter}:"
                
                if ($SourcePath) {
                    # Copy entire media structure
                    Copy-Item -Path "$SourcePath\*" -Destination $bootDrive -Recurse -Force
                }
                else {
                    # Create structure and copy WIM
                    New-Item -Path "$bootDrive\sources" -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                    New-Item -Path "$bootDrive\boot" -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                    New-Item -Path "$bootDrive\EFI\Microsoft\Boot" -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                    New-Item -Path "$bootDrive\EFI\Boot" -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null
                    
                    Copy-Item -Path $WimPath -Destination "$bootDrive\sources\boot.wim" -Force
                    
                    # Copy boot files from ADK
                    $adkPath = Split-Path -Path (Split-Path -Path $script:OscdimgPath -Parent) -Parent
                    $bootFilesPath = Join-Path -Path $adkPath -ChildPath '..\Windows Preinstallation Environment\amd64\Media'
                    
                    if (Test-Path -Path $bootFilesPath) {
                        Copy-Item -Path "$bootFilesPath\*" -Destination $bootDrive -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
                    }
                }
                
                Write-Host "  Step 4: Configuring UEFI boot..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
                
                # Ensure EFI boot files
                $efiBootPath = "$bootDrive\EFI\Boot"
                $efiMsBootPath = "$bootDrive\EFI\Microsoft\Boot"
                
                # Copy bootx64.efi if missing
                if (-not (Test-Path -Path "$efiBootPath\bootx64.efi")) {
                    $sourceEfi = "$env:SystemRoot\Boot\EFI\bootmgfw.efi"
                    if (Test-Path -Path $sourceEfi) {
                        Copy-Item -Path $sourceEfi -Destination "$efiBootPath\bootx64.efi" -Force
                    }
                }
                
                # Run bootsect for BIOS compatibility
                $bootsectPath = Join-Path -Path (Split-Path -Path $script:OscdimgPath -Parent) -ChildPath 'bootsect.exe'
                if (Test-Path -Path $bootsectPath) {
                    & $bootsectPath /nt60 $bootDrive /force /mbr 2>&1 | Out-Null
                }
                
                Write-Host ''
                Write-Host "  ✓ USB drive created successfully!" -ForegroundColor Green
                Write-Host "    Boot Partition: $bootDrive" -ForegroundColor White
                if ($dataLetter) {
                    Write-Host "    Data Partition: ${dataLetter}:" -ForegroundColor White
                }
                Write-Host ''
                
                return [PSCustomObject]@{
                    DiskNumber      = $targetDisk.Number
                    DriveName       = $targetDisk.FriendlyName
                    BootPartition   = $bootDrive
                    DataPartition   = if ($dataLetter) { "${dataLetter}:" } else { $null }
                    PartitionScheme = $PartitionScheme
                    Status          = 'Created'
                    CreatedAt       = Get-Date
                }
            }
        }
        catch {
            Write-Error "Failed to create USB: $_"
            return [PSCustomObject]@{
                DiskNumber = $DiskNumber
                Status     = 'Failed'
                Error      = $_.Exception.Message
            }
        }
    }
}

function New-DualPartitionUSB {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Creates a dual-partition USB drive for large WinPE builds
    .DESCRIPTION
        Creates a USB with FAT32 boot partition (UEFI) and NTFS data partition
        for tools and files larger than 4GB
    .PARAMETER DiskNumber
        Target USB disk number
    .PARAMETER WimPath
        Path to boot.wim
    .PARAMETER BootPartitionSizeGB
        Size of boot partition in GB (default: 1)
    .EXAMPLE
        New-DualPartitionUSB -DiskNumber 2 -WimPath 'C:\boot.wim'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess, ConfirmImpact = 'High')]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
        [int]$DiskNumber,
        
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
        [ValidateScript({ Test-Path -Path $_ -PathType Leaf })]
        [string]$WimPath,
        
        [Parameter()]
        [ValidateRange(1, 32)]
        [int]$BootPartitionSizeGB = 1
    )
    
    process {
        return New-WinPEUSB -DiskNumber $DiskNumber -WimPath $WimPath -PartitionScheme DualPartition
    }
}
#endregion

#region Boot Media Testing
function Test-BootMedia {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Verifies boot media integrity
    .DESCRIPTION
        Checks that ISO or USB contains required boot files
    .PARAMETER Path
        Path to ISO file or USB drive letter
    .EXAMPLE
        Test-BootMedia -Path 'C:\WinPE.iso'
    .EXAMPLE
        Test-BootMedia -Path 'E:'
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
        [string]$Path
    )
    
    process {
        try {
            $isISO = [Path]::GetExtension($Path) -eq '.iso'
            $mountPoint = $null
            
            if ($isISO) {
                # Mount ISO
                $mountResult = Mount-DiskImage -ImagePath $Path -PassThru
                $mountPoint = ($mountResult | Get-Volume).DriveLetter + ':'
            }
            else {
                $mountPoint = $Path.TrimEnd(':') + ':'
            }
            
            try {
                $requiredFiles = @(
                    'sources\boot.wim',
                    'bootmgr',
                    'EFI\Boot\bootx64.efi'
                )
                
                $optionalFiles = @(
                    'boot\bcd',
                    'EFI\Microsoft\Boot\BCD',
                    'boot\boot.sdi'
                )
                
                $results = [List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
                $allRequired = $true
                
                foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
                    $fullPath = Join-Path -Path $mountPoint -ChildPath $file
                    $exists = Test-Path -Path $fullPath
                    if (-not $exists) { $allRequired = $false }
                    
                    $results.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
                        File     = $file
                        Required = $true
                        Exists   = $exists
                        Status   = if ($exists) { '✓' } else { '✗' }
                    })
                }
                
                foreach ($file in $optionalFiles) {
                    $fullPath = Join-Path -Path $mountPoint -ChildPath $file
                    $exists = Test-Path -Path $fullPath
                    
                    $results.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
                        File     = $file
                        Required = $false
                        Exists   = $exists
                        Status   = if ($exists) { '✓' } else { '-' }
                    })
                }
                
                # Check boot.wim info
                $wimPath = Join-Path -Path $mountPoint -ChildPath 'sources\boot.wim'
                $wimInfo = $null
                if (Test-Path -Path $wimPath) {
                    $wimInfo = Get-WindowsImage -ImagePath $wimPath
                }
                
                return [PSCustomObject]@{
                    Path         = $Path
                    Type         = if ($isISO) { 'ISO' } else { 'USB' }
                    Valid        = $allRequired
                    FileChecks   = $results
                    WimInfo      = $wimInfo
                    Status       = if ($allRequired) { 'Valid' } else { 'Invalid - Missing Required Files' }
                }
            }
            finally {
                if ($isISO) {
                    Dismount-DiskImage -ImagePath $Path | Out-Null
                }
            }
        }
        catch {
            Write-Error "Failed to test boot media: $_"
            return [PSCustomObject]@{
                Path   = $Path
                Status = 'Failed'
                Error  = $_.Exception.Message
            }
        }
    }
}
#endregion

Export-ModuleMember -Function *

)
foreach ($dir in $dirs) {

}
Write-Output 'Directory structure created successfully'
Get-ChildItem $base -Recurse -Directory | Select-Object FullName | Format-Table -AutoSize
"

# Skills Master Plan
> **Total Skills:** 17 | **Base Path:** D:\Skills | **Created:** 2026-02-05

---

## Executive Summary

This document defines the complete architecture for 17 custom Claude skills organized into 4 categories. Each skill follows the skill-creator framework with SKILL.md, scripts/, references/, and assets/ as needed.

---

## Skill Inventory

### Category 1: Windows Infrastructure (5 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 1 | Windows Image Master | `windows-image-master` | P0 | High |
| 2 | Windows Automation | `windows-automation` | P0 | Medium |
| 3 | Windows File Organizer | `windows-file-organizer` | P2 | Low |
| 4 | Registry Operations Expert | `registry-ops-expert` | P1 | Medium |
| 5 | Windows Service Creator | `windows-service-creator` | P1 | Medium |

### Category 2: Development Workflow (6 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 6 | MCP Server Creator | `mcp-server-creator` | P0 | Medium |
| 7 | Code Base Analyzer | `codebase-analyzer` | P0 | High |
| 8 | Scope Creep Maintainer | `scope-creep-maintainer` | P1 | Medium |
| 9 | Project Scaffolder | `project-scaffolder` | P0 | Medium |
| 10 | PowerShell Module Scaffolder | `powershell-module-scaffolder` | P0 | Medium |
| 11 | WinUI 3/MVVM Generator | `winui3-mvvm-generator` | P1 | High |

### Category 3: Ideation & Implementation (3 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 12 | Idea/Feature Suggester | `idea-suggester` | P1 | Medium |
| 13 | Idea/Feature Implementer | `idea-implementer` | P1 | High |
| 14 | Idea/Feature Revisor | `idea-revisor` | P2 | Medium |

### Category 4: Quality & DevOps (3 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 15 | Documentation Generator | `documentation-generator` | P1 | Medium |
| 16 | Test Coverage Analyzer | `test-coverage-analyzer` | P1 | Medium |
| 17 | CI/CD Pipeline Builder | `cicd-pipeline-builder` | P2 | High |

---

## Recommended Build Order

### Phase 1: Foundation (P0 - Build First)
1. **Project Scaffolder** - Foundation for all other project-based skills
2. **PowerShell Module Scaffolder** - Enables PS module creation for other skills
3. **MCP Server Creator** - Standalone, high utility
4. **Code Base Analyzer** - Informs other skills' decisions
5. **Windows Automation** - Core Windows capability
6. **Windows Image Master** - Comprehensive Windows imaging

### Phase 2: Enhancement (P1 - Build Second)
7. **Registry Operations Expert** - Supports Windows skills
8. **Windows Service Creator** - Supports Windows automation
9. **WinUI 3/MVVM Generator** - Supports Better11 development
10. **Scope Creep Maintainer** - Project management
11. **Idea Suggester** - Ideation workflow start
12. **Idea Implementer** - Ideation workflow middle
13. **Documentation Generator** - Quality improvement
14. **Test Coverage Analyzer** - Quality improvement

### Phase 3: Polish (P2 - Build Last)
15. **Windows File Organizer** - Utility skill
16. **Idea Revisor** - Ideation workflow end
17. **CI/CD Pipeline Builder** - Advanced DevOps

---

## Skill Dependencies

```
project-scaffolder ──────┬──> powershell-module-scaffolder
                         ├──> winui3-mvvm-generator
                         ├──> windows-service-creator
                         └──> mcp-server-creator

codebase-analyzer ───────┬──> scope-creep-maintainer
                         ├──> idea-suggester
                         ├──> test-coverage-analyzer
                         └──> documentation-generator

windows-automation ──────┬──> windows-image-master
                         ├──> registry-ops-expert
                         └──> windows-file-organizer

idea-suggester ──────────> idea-implementer ──────────> idea-revisor
```

---

### 1. Windows Image Master
**Folder:** `D:\Skills\windows-image-master`

**Structure:**
```

│   ├── Add-TestStage.ps1
│   ├── Add-DeployStage.ps1
│   ├── Validate-Pipeline.ps1
│   └── Migrate-Pipeline.ps1
├── references/
│   ├── github-actions-guide.md
│   ├── azure-devops-guide.md
│   ├── gitlab-ci-guide.md
│   ├── pipeline-patterns.md
│   └── secrets-management.md
└── assets/
    └── templates/
        ├── github-actions/
        │   ├── dotnet-build.yml
        │   ├── powershell-test.yml
        │   └── deploy-azure.yml
        ├── azure-devops/
        │   ├── dotnet-pipeline.yml
        │   └── release-pipeline.yml
        └── gitlab/
            └── .gitlab-ci.yml
```

---

## Next Steps

### Immediate Actions
1. Review and approve this master plan
2. Select first skill to build
3. Begin incremental implementation

### Per-Skill Build Process
1. Initialize skill structure using init_skill.py
2. Create SKILL.md with frontmatter and body
3. Implement scripts with full functionality
4. Create reference documentation
5. Add asset templates
6. Test all scripts (100% coverage, PSScriptAnalyzer clean)
7. Package skill using package_skill.py
8. Validate and iterate

### Quality Gates (Per Skill)
- [ ] SKILL.md complete with triggers
- [ ] All scripts functional
- [ ] PSScriptAnalyzer 0 errors
- [ ] 100% test coverage
- [ ] References documented
- [ ] Assets included
- [ ] Package validates

# Skills Master Plan
> **Total Skills:** 17 | **Base Path:** D:\Skills | **Created:** 2026-02-05

---

## Executive Summary

This document defines the complete architecture for 17 custom Claude skills organized into 4 categories. Each skill follows the skill-creator framework with SKILL.md, scripts/, references/, and assets/ as needed.

---

## Skill Inventory

### Category 1: Windows Infrastructure (5 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 1 | Windows Image Master | `windows-image-master` | P0 | High |
| 2 | Windows Automation | `windows-automation` | P0 | Medium |
| 3 | Windows File Organizer | `windows-file-organizer` | P2 | Low |
| 4 | Registry Operations Expert | `registry-ops-expert` | P1 | Medium |
| 5 | Windows Service Creator | `windows-service-creator` | P1 | Medium |

### Category 2: Development Workflow (6 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 6 | MCP Server Creator | `mcp-server-creator` | P0 | Medium |
| 7 | Code Base Analyzer | `codebase-analyzer` | P0 | High |
| 8 | Scope Creep Maintainer | `scope-creep-maintainer` | P1 | Medium |
| 9 | Project Scaffolder | `project-scaffolder` | P0 | Medium |
| 10 | PowerShell Module Scaffolder | `powershell-module-scaffolder` | P0 | Medium |
| 11 | WinUI 3/MVVM Generator | `winui3-mvvm-generator` | P1 | High |

### Category 3: Ideation & Implementation (3 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 12 | Idea/Feature Suggester | `idea-suggester` | P1 | Medium |
| 13 | Idea/Feature Implementer | `idea-implementer` | P1 | High |
| 14 | Idea/Feature Revisor | `idea-revisor` | P2 | Medium |

### Category 4: Quality & DevOps (3 skills)

| # | Skill | Folder Name | Priority | Complexity |
|---|-------|-------------|----------|------------|
| 15 | Documentation Generator | `documentation-generator` | P1 | Medium |
| 16 | Test Coverage Analyzer | `test-coverage-analyzer` | P1 | Medium |
| 17 | CI/CD Pipeline Builder | `cicd-pipeline-builder` | P2 | High |

---

## Recommended Build Order

### Phase 1: Foundation (P0 - Build First)
1. **Project Scaffolder** - Foundation for all other project-based skills
2. **PowerShell Module Scaffolder** - Enables PS module creation for other skills
3. **MCP Server Creator** - Standalone, high utility
4. **Code Base Analyzer** - Informs other skills' decisions
5. **Windows Automation** - Core Windows capability
6. **Windows Image Master** - Comprehensive Windows imaging

### Phase 2: Enhancement (P1 - Build Second)
7. **Registry Operations Expert** - Supports Windows skills
8. **Windows Service Creator** - Supports Windows automation
9. **WinUI 3/MVVM Generator** - Supports Better11 development
10. **Scope Creep Maintainer** - Project management
11. **Idea Suggester** - Ideation workflow start
12. **Idea Implementer** - Ideation workflow middle
13. **Documentation Generator** - Quality improvement
14. **Test Coverage Analyzer** - Quality improvement

### Phase 3: Polish (P2 - Build Last)
15. **Windows File Organizer** - Utility skill
16. **Idea Revisor** - Ideation workflow end
17. **CI/CD Pipeline Builder** - Advanced DevOps

---

## Skill Dependencies

```
project-scaffolder ──────┬──> powershell-module-scaffolder
                         ├──> winui3-mvvm-generator
                         ├──> windows-service-creator
                         └──> mcp-server-creator

codebase-analyzer ───────┬──> scope-creep-maintainer
                         ├──> idea-suggester
                         ├──> test-coverage-analyzer
                         └──> documentation-generator

windows-automation ──────┬──> windows-image-master
                         ├──> registry-ops-expert
                         └──> windows-file-organizer

idea-suggester ──────────> idea-implementer ──────────> idea-revisor
```

arguments.content:
# Comprehensive Folder Cleanup Plan for C:\Users\C-Man

## Executive Summary

The home directory and especially `OneDrive\Dev` contain massive duplication caused by
OneDrive sync conflicts and repeated artifact extraction. This plan addresses cleanup
in 7 phases, ordered from safest/highest-impact to most nuanced. Every phase uses
dry-run-first methodology and generates deletion manifests before any destructive action.

**Estimated space recovery**: ~15-20 GB (WIM/ISO alone = ~15.5 GB; duplicates = several GB)

---

## PHASE 0: Pre-Flight Safety Checks

### 0A. Pause OneDrive Sync
OneDrive sync conflict resolution is the root cause of `_1`, `_2`, `(2)` suffixed
duplicates. If sync is active during cleanup, files may be recreated.

```powershell
# Check OneDrive sync status
Get-Process OneDrive -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

# Pause OneDrive sync via tray icon (manual step - right-click OneDrive tray icon > Pause syncing > 24 hours)
# OR kill the process temporarily:
# Stop-Process -Name OneDrive -Force
```

**IMPORTANT**: Pause sync BEFORE any deletion in OneDrive\Dev. Resume AFTER cleanup completes.

### 0B. Create Master Log Directory
```powershell
$LogRoot = "C:\Users\C-Man\Documents\CleanupLogs\$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')"
New-Item -Path $LogRoot -ItemType Directory -Force
```

### 0C. Snapshot Current State
```powershell
# Record full directory listing before any changes
Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\C-Man" -Recurse -Depth 1 -Force |
    Select-Object FullName, Length, LastWriteTime, Attributes |
    Export-Csv "$LogRoot\home-snapshot-before.csv" -NoTypeInformation

Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev" -Depth 0 -Force |
    Select-Object FullName, Length, LastWriteTime, Attributes |
    Export-Csv "$LogRoot\onedrivedev-snapshot-before.csv" -NoTypeInformation
```

### 0D. Identify Windows System Junctions - DO NOT TOUCH
These are Windows-managed symlinks/junctions in the home directory. They must NEVER
be deleted:

```
C:\Users\C-Man\Application Data     -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming
C:\Users\C-Man\Cookies              -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows\INetCookies
C:\Users\C-Man\Local Settings       -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Local
C:\Users\C-Man\My Documents         -> C:\Users\C-Man\Documents
C:\Users\C-Man\NetHood              -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Network Shortcuts
C:\Users\C-Man\PrintHood            -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Printer Shortcuts
C:\Users\C-Man\Recent               -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Recent
C:\Users\C-Man\SendTo               -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\SendTo
C:\Users\C-Man\Start Menu           -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu
C:\Users\C-Man\Templates            -> C:\Users\C-Man\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Templates
```

The cleanup scripts must always exclude these using a reparse-point check:
```powershell
$SystemJunctions = @(
    'Application Data', 'Cookies', 'Local Settings', 'My Documents',
    'NetHood', 'PrintHood', 'Recent', 'SendTo', 'Start Menu', 'Templates',
    'OneDrive'
)
```

---

## PHASE 1: OneDrive\Dev - Diagnostics Folders (Highest Volume, Simplest)

**Target**: 45+ `diagnostics_N` folders, each containing only `events.jsonl`
**Pattern**: `diagnostics` (original) + `diagnostics_1` through `diagnostics_72` (duplicates)
**Verified**: Both `diagnostics` and `diagnostics_1` and `diagnostics_72` contain identical
single-file structure (`events.jsonl`).

### 1A. Dry Run - List All Diagnostics Duplicates
```powershell
$DevRoot = "C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev"
$DiagDupes = Get-ChildItem -Path $DevRoot -Directory |
    Where-Object { $_.Name -match '^diagnostics_\d+$' } |
    Sort-Object Name

Write-Host "=== DIAGNOSTICS DUPLICATES (DRY RUN) ==="
Write-Host "Original: $DevRoot\diagnostics"
Write-Host "Duplicates found: $($DiagDupes.Count)"
$DiagDupes | ForEach-Object {
    $contents = Get-ChildItem $_.FullName -Recurse
    Write-Host "  WOULD DELETE: $($_.FullName) (items inside: $($contents.Count))"
}
$DiagDupes | Select-Object FullName, CreationTime |
    Export-Csv "$LogRoot\phase1-diagnostics-dryrun.csv" -NoTypeInformation
```

### 1B. Verify Original Exists and Has Content
```powershell
$OrigDiag = Join-Path $DevRoot "diagnostics"
if (Test-Path $OrigDiag) {
    $origContent = Get-ChildItem $OrigDiag -Recurse
    Write-Host "Original 'diagnostics' exists with $($origContent.Count) item(s)"
    if ($origContent.Count -eq 0) {
        Write-Warning "ORIGINAL IS EMPTY - ABORT PHASE 1. Pick best duplicate instead."
        return
    }
} else {
    Write-Warning "ORIGINAL DOES NOT EXIST - ABORT PHASE 1."
    return
}
```

### 1C. Execute Deletion
```powershell
$DiagDupes | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Removing: $($_.FullName)"
    Remove-Item -Path $_.FullName -Recurse -Force
} 
# Log completion
"Phase 1 complete: Removed $($DiagDupes.Count) diagnostics duplicates" |
    Out-File "$LogRoot\phase1-complete.log"
```

---

## PHASE 2: OneDrive\Dev - Numbered Duplicate Directories (Bulk)

**Target**: All directories matching `OriginalName_N` pattern where the un-suffixed
original exists.

### Duplicate Directory Groups Identified (from exploration):

| Original | Duplicates | Count |
|---|---|---|
| PSColor | PSColor_1, _4, _5, _6, _7, _9, _10, _11 | 8 |
| Aurora.Core (and .Drivers, .Install, .Retry, .Tweaks) | Aurora_1.Core through Aurora_5.Core (x5 each module) | 25 |
| AuroraSuite_Full_Win11_25H2 | _1, _2 | 2 |
| AuroraSuite_Scheduler | _1, _2 | 2 |
| Aurora_Dashboard_Added_20251101_191930 | _1, _2 | 2 |
| Aurora_Lifecycle_Enhancements_20251102_150443 | _1, _2 | 2 |
| Aurora_Prod_Polish_20251101_221721 | _2, _3 | 2 |
| Aurora_scaffold_v12 | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Aurora_scaffold_v4 | _2 | 1 |
| Aurora_scaffold_v7 | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Aurora_Skeleton_20251101_142546 | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Aurora_Updated_20251101_142741 | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Aurora_v2.1_Gamer_MAX | Aurora_v2_1.1, _v2_2.1, _v2_3.1 | 3 |
| Auto-Suite-0.1.0-baseline | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Autoruns | _1, _2 | 2 |
| Batlez.Folder | Batlez_2.Folder, Batlez_3.Folder | 2 |
| Better11-Complete-Suite | _2 | 1 |
| Better11-Unified-Suite-Complete | _1, _2, _3, " 2", " 2_1" | 5 |
| Better11_Home_Edition_v2.0 | _v2_1.0, _v2_2.0 | 2 |
| Better11_Integration_Module_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_5.0 | 5 |
| bootracer_free | _2, _3 | 2 |
| Broker | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| code_inventory | _2 | 1 |
| Complete-Artifacts-Archive | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| COMPLETE_Enterprise_Windows_Toolkit_Suite_v1.0 | _v1_1.0, _v1_2.0 | 2 |
| CustomWinPE_Toolkit_v2.0 | _v2_1.0, _v2_2.0, _v2_3.0 | 3 |
| data-2025-12-27-22-09-06-batch-0000 | _1, _2 | 2 |
| DeployForge-main | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Files-Settings | _2, _3 | 2 |
| GamingOptimizer_AllIn | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Hardware_Inventory_Tool_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_5.0 | 5 |
| Idb | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| LanguagesV10 | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| LGPO | _2, _3 | 2 |
| md-aspnetcore | _2, _3 | 2 |
| md-csharp | _2 | 1 |
| md-vb_1 | _2, _3 (note: no bare "md-vb" original) | special |
| md-vc | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| md-web | _1, _2 | 2 |
| Microsoft Edge v139 Security Baseline | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Microsoft.ML.ModelBuilder.CSharpItemTemplate | _2, _3 | 2 |
| Microsoft.ML.ModelBuilder.WebItemTemplate | _2, _3 | 2 |
| missing_directory | _2 | 1 |
| nested_portion1 | _2 | 1 |
| PolicyAnalyzer | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| PowerShellProfile-files | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| PublishConfiguration | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| RuleSet | _1, _2, _3, _4, _5 | 5 |
| schemastore | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| SetObjectSecurity | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| System_Image_Management_Suite_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_6.0 | 6 |
| tbosdts | _1, _2, _3, _4, _5 | 5 |
| tbosdts_en | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| TestWindowRemoteAgent | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| top_level_portion1 | _1, _2 | 2 |
| UltraProfile_Phase3_patch | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| UltraProfile_Phase3_RTX_Games_patch | _2, _3 | 2 |
| VCPkgHeadersDatabase | _1, _2 | 2 |
| ventoy-1.1.07-windows | ventoy-1.1_1.07, _1.1_2.07, _1.1_3.07 | 3 |
| VG278Q_WHQL_Driver | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Windows 11 v24H2 Security Baseline | _1, _2, _3 | 3 |
| Windows_Deployment_System_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_6.0 | 6 |
| Windows_Driver_Package_Manager_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_6.0 | 6 |
| Windows_Update_Management_v1.0 | _v1_1.0 through _v1_5.0 | 5 |
| windick-main | _2, _3 | 2 |
| zipdir | _1, _2 | 2 |
| zipdir_backslash | _1, _2 | 2 |
| zip_cp437_header | _1, _2 | 2 |
| files_2, files_3 | (need to check if bare "files" exists) | special |

**Total estimated duplicate directories: ~200+**

### 2A. Dry Run - Generic Duplicate Directory Finder
```powershell
$DevRoot = "C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev"
$AllDirs = Get-ChildItem -Path $DevRoot -Directory

# Pattern: name ends with _N where N is one or more digits
$DupeDirs = $AllDirs | Where-Object {
    $_.Name -match '^(.+?)_(\d+)$' -or          # underscored: Name_1, Name_2
    $_.Name -match '^(.+?)_(\d+)\.(.+)$'         # underscored with dot: Name_1.Ext
}

# Also match space-number pattern: "Name 2", "Name 2_1"  
$SpaceDupeDirs = $AllDirs | Where-Object {
    $_.Name -match '^(.+?)\s+(\d+)$' -or         # "Name 2"
    $_.Name -match '^(.+?)\s+(\d+)_(\d+)$'       # "Name 2_1"
}

# Combine and deduplicate
$AllDupeCandidates = @($DupeDirs) + @($SpaceDupeDirs) | Sort-Object FullName -Unique

# For each candidate, derive the original name and check if it exists
$RemovalPlan = @()
foreach ($dir in $AllDupeCandidates) {
    # Skip diagnostics (handled in Phase 1)
    if ($dir.Name -match '^diagnostics_\d+$') { continue }
    
    # Derive original name by stripping suffix patterns
    $originalName = $dir.Name
    # Handle "_N" suffix (e.g., PSColor_1 -> PSColor)
    if ($originalName -match '^(.+?)_(\d+)$') {
        $originalName = $Matches[1]
    }
    # Handle "_N.Ext" suffix (e.g., Aurora_1.Core -> Aurora.Core)  
    elseif ($originalName -match '^(.+?)_(\d+)\.(.+)$') {
        $originalName = "$($Matches[1]).$($Matches[3])"
    }
    # Handle " N" suffix (e.g., "Better11-Unified-Suite-Complete 2")
    elseif ($originalName -match '^(.+?)\s+\d+(_\d+)?$') {
        $originalName = $Matches[1]
    }

    $originalPath = Join-Path $DevRoot $originalName
    
    if (Test-Path $originalPath) {
        $RemovalPlan += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Duplicate    = $dir.FullName
            Original     = $originalPath
            OrigExists   = $true
            DupeSize     = (Get-ChildItem $dir.FullName -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
                           Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum).Sum
        }
    } else {
        $RemovalPlan += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Duplicate    = $dir.FullName
            Original     = $originalPath
            OrigExists   = $false
            DupeSize     = $null
        }
    }
}

# Report
Write-Host "`n=== DUPLICATE DIRECTORIES DRY RUN ==="
Write-Host "Total candidates: $($RemovalPlan.Count)"
Write-Host "`nSAFE TO REMOVE (original exists):"
$SafeToRemove = $RemovalPlan | Where-Object { $_.OrigExists -eq $true }
$SafeToRemove | ForEach-Object {
    $sizeMB = [math]::Round($_.DupeSize / 1MB, 2)
    Write-Host "  WOULD DELETE: $($_.Duplicate) ($sizeMB MB)"
}
Write-Host "`nNEEDS REVIEW (original NOT found):"
$NeedsReview = $RemovalPlan | Where-Object { $_.OrigExists -eq $false }
$NeedsReview | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  REVIEW: $($_.Duplicate) -> expected original: $($_.Original)"
}

# Export manifest
$RemovalPlan | Export-Csv "$LogRoot\phase2-directories-dryrun.csv" -NoTypeInformation
```

### 2B. Special Cases Requiring Manual Review
These items do NOT have a clean "original" and need manual decision:

1. **`md-vb_1`, `md-vb_2`, `md-vb_3`** - No bare `md-vb` exists. Keep `md-vb_1` as the "original", delete `_2` and `_3`.
2. **`files_2`, `files_3`** - Need to check if a `files` directory exists.
3. **`Aurora_Prod_Polish_20251101_221721_2`, `_3`** - No `_1` variant; original has no suffix. Safe.
4. **`hashes_*` directories** - These follow a different pattern: `hashes_NAME_DATE` with `_2`, `_3` suffixes. The original has no numeric suffix. Safe to remove numbered variants.

### 2C. Execute Safe Removals
```powershell
$SafeToRemove | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Removing: $($_.Duplicate)"
    Remove-Item -Path $_.Duplicate -Recurse -Force
}
"Phase 2 complete: Removed $($SafeToRemove.Count) duplicate directories" |
    Out-File "$LogRoot\phase2-complete.log"
```

---

## PHASE 3: OneDrive\Dev - Duplicate Files (Scripts, Docs, ZIPs)

**Target**: Files with ` 1`, ` 2`, `(2)`, `-1` suffixes that have matching originals.

### Verified Duplicates (same size = confirmed identical):
| Original | Duplicate | Size |
|---|---|---|
| Archive.zip | Archive-1.zip | 383,434 bytes each |
| Better11-Complete.zip | Better11-Complete 1.zip | 50,060 bytes each |
| All-Projects-Master-Bundle.zip | All-Projects-Master-Bundle 1.zip | 85,225 bytes each |
| Better11-Complete-Export.zip | Better11-Complete-Export 1.zip | (to verify) |
| WIDT-Toolkit.zip | WIDT-Toolkit 1.zip | (to verify) |
| WinPE-PowerBuilder-v25.zip | WinPE-PowerBuilder-v25 1.zip | (to verify) |
| Better11_Ok_Module_ULTIMATE.zip | Better11_Ok_Module_ULTIMATE 1.zip | (to verify) |
| unattend.iso | unattend 1.iso | (to verify) |

### Duplicate Script Files:
```
WinPE-CreateUSB.ps1          / WinPE-CreateUSB 1.ps1
WinPE-CustomizeWinRE.ps1     / WinPE-CustomizeWinRE 1.ps1 / WinPE-CustomizeWinRE 2.ps1
WinPE-Detect.ps1             / WinPE-Detect 1.ps1
WinPE-InjectDrivers.ps1      / WinPE-InjectDrivers 1.ps1
WinPE-InjectUpdates.ps1      / WinPE-InjectUpdates 1.ps1 / WinPE-InjectUpdates 2.ps1
WinPE-Mount.ps1              / WinPE-Mount 1.ps1 / WinPE-Mount 2.ps1
WinPE-Toolkit.ps1            / WinPE-Toolkit 1.ps1 / WinPE-Toolkit 2.ps1
WinPE-Unmount.ps1            / WinPE-Unmount 1.ps1 / WinPE-Unmount 2.ps1
SystemOptimization.psd1      / SystemOptimization 1.psd1
SystemOptimization.psm1      / SystemOptimization 1.psm1
```

### Duplicate Documents:
```
INDEX.md                     / INDEX 1.md / INDEX (2).md
README.md                    / README 1.md / README 2.md / README (2).md
QUICKSTART.md                / QUICKSTART 1.md / QUICKSTART (2).md
QUICK_REFERENCE.md           / QUICK_REFERENCE 1.md
PROJECT_SUMMARY.md           / PROJECT_SUMMARY 1.md / PROJECT_SUMMARY (2).md
SESSION_3_SUMMARY.md         / SESSION_3_SUMMARY 1.md
Configuration-Guide.md       / Configuration-Guide 1.md
All-Code-Bundle-Index.md     / All-Code-Bundle-Index 1.md
```

### Duplicate C# Source Files:
```
ISystemOptimizationService.cs   / ISystemOptimizationService 1.cs / ISystemOptimizationService 2.cs
SystemOptimizationService.cs    / SystemOptimizationService 1.cs / SystemOptimizationService 2.cs
SystemOptimizationViewModel.cs  / SystemOptimizationViewModel 1.cs / SystemOptimizationViewModel 2.cs
OptimizationModels.cs           / OptimizationModels 1.cs
```

### 3A. Dry Run - Duplicate File Finder
```powershell
$DevRoot = "C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev"
$AllFiles = Get-ChildItem -Path $DevRoot -File

# Pattern: "Name N.ext" or "Name (N).ext" or "Name-N.ext" (where N is a number)
$DupeFiles = @()

foreach ($file in $AllFiles) {
    $baseName = $file.BaseName
    $ext = $file.Extension
    $originalName = $null
    
    # Pattern: "Name 1" -> "Name"
    if ($baseName -match '^(.+?)\s+\d+$') {
        $originalName = "$($Matches[1])$ext"
    }
    # Pattern: "Name (2)" -> "Name"
    elseif ($baseName -match '^(.+?)\s+\(\d+\)$') {
        $originalName = "$($Matches[1])$ext"
    }
    # Pattern: "Name-1" for zip files specifically (Archive-1.zip)
    elseif ($baseName -match '^(.+?)-(\d+)$' -and $ext -in '.zip','.iso') {
        $originalName = "$($Matches[1])$ext"
    }
    
    if ($originalName) {
        $originalPath = Join-Path $DevRoot $originalName
        $origExists = Test-Path $originalPath
        $sizeMatch = $false
        if ($origExists) {
            $origSize = (Get-Item $originalPath).Length
            $dupeSize = $file.Length
            $sizeMatch = ($origSize -eq $dupeSize)
        }
        
        $DupeFiles += [PSCustomObject]@{
            DuplicateFile = $file.FullName
            OriginalFile  = $originalPath
            OrigExists    = $origExists
            SizeMatch     = $sizeMatch
            DupeSize      = $file.Length
        }
    }
}

Write-Host "=== DUPLICATE FILES DRY RUN ==="
Write-Host "SAFE (original exists + same size):"
$DupeFiles | Where-Object { $_.OrigExists -and $_.SizeMatch } | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  WOULD DELETE: $($_.DuplicateFile)"
}
Write-Host "`nSAFE (original exists, different size - HASH CHECK NEEDED):"
$DupeFiles | Where-Object { $_.OrigExists -and -not $_.SizeMatch } | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  REVIEW: $($_.DuplicateFile) (sizes differ)"
}
Write-Host "`nUNSAFE (no original):"
$DupeFiles | Where-Object { -not $_.OrigExists } | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  SKIP: $($_.DuplicateFile)"
}

$DupeFiles | Export-Csv "$LogRoot\phase3-files-dryrun.csv" -NoTypeInformation
```

### 3B. Hash Verification for Same-Size Files
```powershell
$SafeFiles = $DupeFiles | Where-Object { $_.OrigExists -and $_.SizeMatch }
$HashVerified = @()

foreach ($item in $SafeFiles) {
    $origHash = (Get-FileHash $item.OriginalFile -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
    $dupeHash = (Get-FileHash $item.DuplicateFile -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
    $HashVerified += [PSCustomObject]@{
        DuplicateFile = $item.DuplicateFile
        OriginalFile  = $item.OriginalFile
        HashMatch     = ($origHash -eq $dupeHash)
    }
}

# Only delete hash-confirmed duplicates
$ConfirmedDupes = $HashVerified | Where-Object { $_.HashMatch }
$ConfirmedDupes | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Removing confirmed duplicate: $($_.DuplicateFile)"
    Remove-Item $_.DuplicateFile -Force
}
```

---

## PHASE 4: OneDrive\Dev - Empty and Placeholder Directories

**Target**: Empty directories and placeholder folders.

### Confirmed Empty:
- `C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev\untitled folder` (verified empty)
- `C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev\untitled folder 1` (contains Better11 files - NOT empty, review needed)

### 4A. Find All Empty Directories in Dev
```powershell
$DevRoot = "C:\Users\C-Man\OneDrive\Dev"
$EmptyDirs = Get-ChildItem -Path $DevRoot -Directory -Recurse |
    Where-Object { (Get-ChildItem $_.FullName -Force).Count -eq 0 }

Write-Host "=== EMPTY DIRECTORIES ==="
$EmptyDirs | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  WOULD DELETE: $($_.FullName)" }
```

---

## Safety Checklist

- [ ] OneDrive sync paused before starting
- [ ] Before-snapshots created
- [ ] System junctions in home root are NEVER touched
- [ ] Every phase runs dry-run FIRST and exports CSV manifest
- [ ] Hash verification used for file duplicates before deletion
- [ ] Original existence confirmed before any duplicate removal
- [ ] Special cases (md-vb, untitled folder 1) reviewed manually
- [ ] WIM/ISO decision made by user (move vs delete)
- [ ] Temp file cleanup limited to files older than 7 days
- [ ] After-snapshots created and compared
- [ ] OneDrive sync resumed after cleanup

## Estimated Impact

| Phase | Items Removed | Space Saved (est.) |
|---|---|---|
| Phase 1: diagnostics | ~45 folders | ~50 MB |
| Phase 2: duplicate dirs | ~200 folders | ~2-5 GB |
| Phase 3: duplicate files | ~40 files | ~500 MB |
| Phase 4: empty dirs | ~5-10 dirs | negligible |
| Phase 5: home root | WIM/ISO move: 15.5 GB; backups: ~1 MB | 15.5 GB (moved) |
| Phase 6: downloads | ~3 files | ~80 KB |
| Phase 7: AppData/temp | ~1,300+ files | ~200 MB - 1 GB |
| **TOTAL** | **~1,600+ items** | **~18-22 GB** |

## Execution Order

1. Phase 0 (pre-flight) - ALWAYS FIRST
2. Phase 1 (diagnostics) - highest confidence, easiest win
3. Phase 4 (empty dirs) - zero-risk
4. Phase 3 (duplicate files) - with hash verification
5. Phase 2 (duplicate dirs) - largest impact, review special cases
6. Phase 6 (downloads) - quick cleanup
7. Phase 5 (home root) - requires user decisions on WIM/ISO
8. Phase 7 (AppData) - lowest priority, most risk of side effects
9. Phase 8 (verification) - ALWAYS LAST

Add-Type -AssemblyName PresentationFramework

# Ensure a WPF Application object exists
if (-not [System.Windows.Application]::Current) {
    $app = New-Object System.Windows.Application
}

function Import-GUITheme {
    param([string]$ThemeName)

    $themePath = "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\GUI\Themes\$ThemeName.xaml"

    if (-Not (Test-Path $themePath)) {
        Write-Host "Theme file not found: $themePath"
        return
    }

    $resourceDict = New-Object System.Windows.ResourceDictionary
    $resourceDict.Source = $themePath

    # Apply the theme
    [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries.Clear()
    [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries.Add($resourceDict)

    Write-Host "Theme switched to $ThemeName"
}

function LaunchBuilderGUI {

    $Window           = New-Object System.Windows.Window
    $Window.Title     = "Ultimate PE/RE/Hybrid Build System"
    $Window.Height    = 560
    $Window.Width     = 720
    $Window.Background = "#1E1E1E"
    $Window.WindowStartupLocation = "CenterScreen"

    $Grid = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Grid
    $Window.Content = $Grid
    $Grid.Margin = 20

    $title = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.TextBlock
    $title.Text = "Ultimate Build Console"
    $title.FontSize = 28
    $title.HorizontalAlignment = "Center"
    $title.Foreground = "White"
    $title.Margin = "0,10,0,20"
    $Grid.Children.Add($title)

    $buttons = @(
        "Build WinPE",
        "Build WinRE",
        "Build Hybrid",
        "Build Combined WIM",
        "Build Multi-Boot ISO",
        "Run Full Build",
        "Open Output Folder",
        "Exit"
    )

    $row = 1
    foreach ($label in $buttons) {

        $Grid.RowDefinitions.Add((New-Object System.Windows.Controls.RowDefinition))

        $btn = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Button
        $btn.Content = $label
        $btn.Height = 50
        $btn.FontSize = 18
        $btn.Background = "#353535"
        $btn.Foreground = "White"
        $btn.Margin = "0,5,0,5"
        $btn.Tag = $label

        $btn.Add_Click({
            switch ($this.Tag) {
                "Build WinPE"          { Build-WinPE }
                "Build WinRE"          { Build-WinRE }
                "Build Hybrid"         { Build-Hybrid }
                "Build Combined WIM"   { Build-Combined }
                "Build Multi-Boot ISO" { Build-ISO }
                "Run Full Build"       { Build-All }
                "Open Output Folder"   { Start-Process "D:\My-Win[PE][RE]\Output" }
                "Exit"                 { $Window.Close() }
            }
        })

        $Grid.Children.Add($btn)
        [System.Windows.Controls.Grid]::SetRow($btn,$row)
        $row++
    }

    $Window.ShowDialog()
}

if (-not [System.Windows.Application]::Current) {
    $app = New-Object System.Windows.Application
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# LOAD ENGINE MODULES (Paths → Logger → Others)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Paths.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Logger.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Progress.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Downloader.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Drivers.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\WimBuilder.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\ISOBuilder.psm1" -Force
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\BuildEngine\Diagnostics.psm1" -Force

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# LOAD GUI STYLES + APPLY DEFAULT THEME
# ---------------------------------------------------------
Import-Module "D:\Win-PERE-BuilderSuite\GUI\GUI-Styles.psm1" -Force
Import-GUITheme -ThemeName "Dark"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# CREATE MAIN WINDOW
# ---------------------------------------------------------

$Window                   = New-Object System.Windows.Window
$Window.Title             = "Win‑PERE Builder Suite"
$Window.Width             = 1024
$Window.Height            = 640
$Window.WindowStartupLocation = "CenterScreen"

# Apply background after ResourceDictionary exists
$Window.Background        = [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources["WindowBackgroundBrush"]

# Main grid
$Grid        = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Grid
$Grid.Margin = "12"
$Window.Content = $Grid

# Columns
$Grid.ColumnDefinitions.Add( (New-Object System.Windows.Controls.ColumnDefinition) )
$Grid.ColumnDefinitions.Add( (New-Object System.Windows.Controls.ColumnDefinition) )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# LEFT PANEL BUTTON STACK
# ---------------------------------------------------------

$ButtonPanel = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.StackPanel
$ButtonPanel.Margin = "6"
$ButtonPanel.VerticalAlignment = "Top"

$Grid.Children.Add($ButtonPanel)
[System.Windows.Controls.Grid]::SetColumn($ButtonPanel,0)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# BUTTON CREATOR FUNCTION (APPLIES THEME BRUSHES)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

function New-GUIButton {
    param(
        [string]$Text,
        [scriptblock]$Action
    )

    $btn = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Button
    $btn.Content  = $Text
    $btn.FontSize = 18
    $btn.Height   = 48
    $btn.Margin   = "0,6,0,6"

    # THEME‑SAFE BRUSHES
    $btn.Foreground   = [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources["ForegroundBrush"]
    $btn.Background   = [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources["ButtonBackgroundBrush"]
    $btn.BorderBrush  = [System.Windows.Application]::Current.Resources["ButtonBorderBrush"]

    $btn.Add_Click({
        try {
            & $Action
        }
        catch {
            Write-Log "GUI Action Failed: $_" "ERROR"
        }
    })

    return $btn
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ADD ACTION BUTTONS
# ---------------------------------------------------------

$ButtonPanel.Children.Add( (New-GUIButton "Build WinPE"          { Build-WinPE }) )
$ButtonPanel.Children.Add( (New-GUIButton "Build WinRE"          { Build-WinRE }) )

[Reading 79 lines from start (total: 79 lines, 0 remaining)]

Add-Type -AssemblyName PresentationFramework

function Start-GUI {
    # Create Window
    $Window = New-Object System.Windows.Window
    $Window.Title = "Ultimate PE/RE/Hybrid Build Console"
    $Window.Height = 530
    $Window.Width = 700
    $Window.WindowStartupLocation = "CenterScreen"
    $Window.ResizeMode = "NoResize"
    $Window.Background = "#1e1e1e"

    # Grid Container
    $Grid = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Grid
    $Grid.Margin = 20
    $Window.Content = $Grid

    # Title
    $Title = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.TextBlock
    $Title.Text = "Ultimate Build System"
    $Title.FontSize = 26
    $Title.Foreground = "White"
    $Title.HorizontalAlignment = "Center"
    $Title.Margin = "0,10,0,20"
    $Grid.Children.Add($Title)

    # Create Buttons
    $buttonLabels = @(
        "Build WinPE",
        "Build WinRE",
        "Build Hybrid",
        "Build Combined WIM",
        "Make Multi-Boot ISO",
        "Run Full Build Process",
        "Open Output Folder",
        "Exit"
    )

    $row = 1

    foreach ($label in $buttonLabels) {

        # Row allocation
        $Grid.RowDefinitions.Add((New-Object System.Windows.Controls.RowDefinition))

        $btn = New-Object System.Windows.Controls.Button
        $btn.Content = $label
        $btn.Margin = "0,5,0,5"
        $btn.FontSize = 18
        $btn.Height = 45
        $btn.Foreground = "White"
        $btn.Background = "#444"
        $btn.Tag = $label

        $btn.Add_Click({
            param($sender,$args)
            $task = $sender.Tag

            switch ($task) {
                "Build WinPE"          { Build-WinPE }
                "Build WinRE"          { Build-WinRE }
                "Build Hybrid"         { Build-Hybrid }
                "Build Combined WIM"   { Build-Combined }
                "Make Multi-Boot ISO"  { Build-ISO }
                "Run Full Build Process" { Build-All }
                "Open Output Folder"   { Start-Process "D:\My-Win[PE][RE]\Output" }
                "Exit"                 { $Window.Close() }
            }
        })

        $Grid.Children.Add($btn)
        [System.Windows.Controls.Grid]::SetRow($btn,$row)
        $row++
    }

    $Window.ShowDialog()
}

Start-GUI

arguments.command: Write-Host '=== PHASE 7: APPDATA CLEANUP ==='; Write-Host '--- 7A: Temp files older than 7 days ---'; $TempPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Temp"; $oldFiles = Get-ChildItem $TempPath -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt (Get-Date).AddDays(-7) }; $totalSize = ($oldFiles | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Sum; Write-Host "Found $($oldFiles.Count) old temp files ($([math]::Round($totalSize/1MB,1)) MB)"; $removedCount = 0; $oldFiles | ForEach-Object { try { Remove-Item $_.FullName -Force -ErrorAction Stop; $removedCount++ } catch {} }; Write-Host "Removed $removedCount temp files"; Write-Host '--- 7B: Claude CWD temp files ---'; $claudeTemps = Get-ChildItem $TempPath -Directory -Filter 'claude-*' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host "Found $($claudeTemps.Count) claude temp entries"; $claudeRemoved = 0; $claudeTemps | ForEach-Object { try { Remove-Item $_.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction Stop; $claudeRemoved++ } catch {} }; Write-Host "Removed $claudeRemoved claude temp entries"; Write-Host '--- 7C: Old Claude Desktop app versions ---'; $claudeAppBase = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\AnthropicClaude"; if (Test-Path $claudeAppBase) { $oldVersions = @('app-1.1.1520','app-1.1.1890'); $oldVersions | ForEach-Object { $vPath = Join-Path $claudeAppBase $_; if (Test-Path $vPath) { Remove-Item $vPath -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host "  Removed old Claude version: $_" } else { Write-Host "  Not found: $_" } } } else { Write-Host 'Claude Desktop app path not found' }; Write-Host 'Phase 7 complete'; Write-Host '---DONE---'
